data.title: Coleman 5-Gallon Solar Shower
data.description: Don't miss a shower  even when you're camping  with the Coleman 5-Gallon Solar Shower. The reservoir holds up to 5 gallons of waterenough for multiple showers. It's constructed of heat-locking material that warms the water when it's left in the sun, even on cool days. The shower features a strong handle for reliable hanging and carrying as well as a handy on/off shower head valve to conserve water.
data.reviewText:
It's inexpensive.....and cheap.  Works OK if (a big IF) you don't mind that the plug isn't tight, so almost anything you do with it will cause water to come out of it.  Other solar powered bags have plugs that screw on, which makes the unit much more leak-proof.

Bought one.  Will go back to others.  They cost a bit more, but are not as messy.
data.text:
TITLE:
Coleman 5-Gallon Solar Shower

DESCRIPTION:
Don't miss a shower  even when you're camping  with the Coleman 5-Gallon Solar Shower. The reservoir holds up to 5 gallons of waterenough for multiple showers. It's constructed of heat-locking material that warms the water when it's left in the sun, even on cool days. The shower features a strong handle for reliable hanging and carrying as well as a handy on/off shower head valve to conserve water.

REVIEW:
It's inexpensive.....and cheap.  Works OK if (a big IF) you don't mind that the plug isn't tight, so almost anything you do with it will cause water to come out of it.  Other solar powered bags have plugs that screw on, which makes the unit much more leak-proof.

Bought one.  Will go back to others.  They cost a bit more, but are not as messy.

data.title: Coleman Tent Kit
data.description: Set up the tent quickly with the steel tent pegs and rubber mallet. Keep it clean for the entire campout with the nesting hand broom and dustpan. When the fun is done, use the tent stake puller to get the pegs out of the ground easily. All four of these important tools fit nicely inside the mesh carry bag for the next adventure.
data.reviewText: Nice for clean up.
data.text:
TITLE:
Coleman Tent Kit

DESCRIPTION:
Set up the tent quickly with the steel tent pegs and rubber mallet. Keep it clean for the entire campout with the nesting hand broom and dustpan. When the fun is done, use the tent stake puller to get the pegs out of the ground easily. All four of these important tools fit nicely inside the mesh carry bag for the next adventure.

REVIEW:
Nice for clean up.

data.title: Coleman 48 Can Party Stacker Cooler
data.description: With the Coleman Party Stacker-Inchs unique stackable, color-coded design, you-Inchll want more than one for parties, tailgating, BBQs and more. The Party Stacker features a clever interlocking base and lid, so you can stack multiple coolers with no slipping or sliding -- great for easy transporting and tidy storage. The Party Stackers come in four great colors, so you can separate drinks or dishes by cooler color. The 33-Quart Party Stacker accommodates two cases of twelve-ounce cans or one case of bottles or longnecks plus ice -- and it-Inchs wide enough for a 9-Inchx13-Inch casserole dish. The Party Stacker features tough molded handles for comfortable lifting and carrying, with a sleek, low-profile design for stability. Exterior measures 22-InchL x 13.5-InchW x 11.75-InchH. Made in the USA.
data.reviewText: The lid isn't great and doesn't seem to stay cool that long.

data.title: Coleman Stratus Adult Fleece Sleeping Bag Liner
data.description:
The Coleman Stratus Adult Fleece Sleeping Bag Liner doubles as both a warm, cozy liner and a low-profile sleeping bag. On its own, the liner can keep you warm in temperatures as low as 50 degrees Fahrenheit. When placed inside another sleeping bag, it can provide 12 degrees Fahrenheit of extra thermal protection. This low-profile sleeping bag liner features a two-way patented ZipPlow zipper system that plows fabric away from the zipper to prevent snags and frustration. It also features an elastic storage strap and stuff sack for easy packing and transport. The Stratus Fleece Sleeping Bag Liner can accommodate most people up to 5 feet 11 inches in height. It is machine washable for easy cleaning. The Coleman 8197C410 Stratus Fleece Sleeping Bag Liner is the perfect accompaniment to any sleeping bag, especially when you are camping where you are not sure how cold it is going to be. This sleeping-bag liner is full-size, measuring 33-inches by 75-inches, and fits most full-size sleeping bags up to 5-feet 11-inches long. When used with a sleeping bag, this liner adds 12 degrees of thermal protection, making a 35-degree sleeping bag usable at temperatures as low as 23 degrees! The Coleman fleece sleeping-bag liner is commercial-machine-washable only and comes with its own stuff sack. Manufacturer Warranty
Five-year warranty
data.reviewText: The zipper is a problem. The dual zipper system is apparently not intuitive. Twice I have had people using the bag end up with the zipper jammed and broken.  I like the bag, it is perfect for warm climate camping here in Florida, but I need somethning that does not require instructions to use.

data.title: Coleman Camping Coffee Maker
data.description: Easily make drip-brewed coffee while enjoying the outdoors with the Coleman Camping Coffee Maker. Designed with a steel base that fits over most 2- and 3-burner camp stoves, this camping coffee maker features a large reservoir that enables you to brew up to 10 cups of hot coffee at once. Making coffee with this outdoor coffee maker is easy. Simply spoon grounds into the removable swing-out filter basket, fill the reservoir with water, and place the coffee maker over your stove burner. Within minutes, the 10-cup glass carafe will be filled with freshly brewed coffee ready to be enjoyed in the great outdoors. A Pause 'N Serve feature lets you grab a cup before the brew cycle is finished. The Coleman Camping Coffee Maker measures 8.5 x 11 x 14 inches. About Coleman More than 100 years ago, a young man with an entrepreneurial spirit and a better idea began manufacturing lanterns in Wichita, Kansas. His name was W.C. Coleman, and the company he founded would change life in America. A man plagued with such poor vision he sometimes had to ask classmates to read aloud to him, Coleman saw a brilliant light in 1900 in a drugstore window that stopped him in his tracks. He inquired about the light inside and discovered he was able to read even the small print on a medicine bottle by the illumination. The lamps had mantles, not wicks, and were fueled by gasoline under pressure instead of coal oil. Soon afterward, Coleman started a lighting service that offered a "no light, no pay" clause--a big step forward for merchants who were burned by inferior products that rarely worked--and drew substantial interest from businesses that wanted to keep their lights on after dark. In the ensuing years, Coleman expanded its product line well beyond lanterns. The company's current catalog is thick with products that make spending time outdoors a pleasure. There are coolers that keep food and drinks cold for days on end, comfortable airbeds that won't deflate during the night, a complete line of LED lights that last for years, powerful portable grills that cook with an authentic open-grill flame, and much, much more. Coleman has truly fashioned much of our outdoor camping experience, and expects to do so for generations to come. END
data.reviewText: Filter basket would not stay shut got a replacement and it had the same problem also the stop dripping function did not work on both I have one that works good  that I have used for a few years now so that is why 3 stars

data.title: Coleman 100-Quart Xtreme 5-Day Heavy-Duty Cooler with Wheels, Blue
data.description:
Food, drinksand the funlast longer, when you roll in with the Coleman 100-Quart Xtreme 5-Day Heavy-Duty Cooler with Wheels. To be exact, this cooler keeps ice up to 5 days at temps as high as 90F thanks to an insulated lid and extra insulation in the walls. Large enough to hold 160 cans, this spacious cooler transports and chills enough food and drinks so the entire party remains fueled and refreshed for the duration of your adventures. Wherever youre headedcampgrounds, tailgate parking lot, or across a ball fieldthe heavy-duty wheels and large tow handle let you roll over almost any terrain with little effort. The additional 2-way side handles make it easier to lift your cooler in and out of the car or onto a picnic bench. Once you arrive, the Have-A-Seat lid ensures you have a place to sit and relax, and the cup holders molded into the lid keep your drink close and prevent spills. When the fun is done, the leak-resistant channel drain lets you empty any excess water without tilting the cooler, so youre ready for your next fun adventure in no time. Carry up to 160 cans with ease--and store ice for up to five days even when it's 90 degrees outside--in the Xtreme 5 100-Quart Wheeled Cooler by Coleman. With two large wheels, two-way handles and a comfortable tow handle for one-hand operation, this cooler is easy to roll even on rough terrain. After the party, it's easy to drain thanks to a rustproof, leak-resistant channel drain for no-tilt draining. Features:
  • Keeps ice up to 5 days at 90 degrees Fahrenheit
  • Holds 160 cans
  • Hinged lid with beverage holders
  • Two-way handles for flexible easy carrying
  • Comfortable tow handle for one-hand operation
  • Rustproof, leak-resistant channel drain for no-tilt easy draining
  • Two large wheels for easy pulling, even on rough terrain
  • Made with Thermozone insulation that contains no toxic CFCs, HFCs, or HCFCs
Specifications:
  • Exterior Dimensions: 36.5 by 16.75 by 18.25 inches
  • Interior Dimensions: 23.75 by 11.75 by 13 inches
  • Weight Empty: 18 pounds
  • Made in the USA
About Coleman
More than 100 years ago, a young man with an entrepreneurial spirit and a better idea began manufacturing lanterns in Wichita, Kansas. His name was W.C. Coleman, and the company he founded would change life in America. A man plagued with such poor vision he sometimes had to ask classmates to read aloud to him, Coleman saw a brilliant light in 1900 in a drugstore window that stopped him in his tracks. He inquired about the light inside and discovered he was able to read even the small print on a medicine bottle by the illumination. The lamps had mantles, not wicks, and were fueled by gasoline under pressure instead of coal oil. Soon afterward, Coleman started a lighting service that offered a "no light, no pay" clause--a big step forward for merchants who were burned by inferior products that rarely worked--and drew substantial interest from businesses that wanted to keep their lights on after dark. In the ensuing years, Coleman expanded its product line well beyond lanterns. The company's current catalog is thick with products that make spending time outdoors a pleasure. There are coolers that keep food and drinks cold for days on end, comfortable airbeds that won't deflate during the night, a complete line of LED lights that last for years, powerful portable grills that cook with an authentic open-grill flame, and much, much more. Coleman has truly fashioned much of our outdoor camping experience, and expects to do so for generations to come.

data.title: Coleman Pack-Away Deluxe Portable Kitchen
data.description:
Bring a cooking station with full features to your next outdoor gathering with the Coleman Pack-Away Deluxe Portable Kitchen. Equipped with a 39.4 x 19.7-inch food prep area, this portable camp kitchen offers ample space for slicing meats and chopping vegetables. A removable sink easily slots into the prep area for easy cleaning. The side stand accommodates most Coleman stoves and coolers, while hanging hooks, a paper-towel holder, and wire shelves offer additional storage space for plates, utensils, and ingredients. To facilitate late-night cooking, a lantern hook provides a convenient place to hang a light. Setting up this portable kitchen is easy: Simply expand the aluminum frame, snap on the surfaces, and make sure the kitchen's footing is steady. When it's time to pack up, the kitchen folds up and easily fits into a single, self-contained case for easy carrying and storage. With its lightweight aluminum frame and sturdy construction, this folding camp kitchen is built to withstand years of rugged use. Outdoor cooking is a ton of fun, but it's not always easy to prep your meal on coolers, picnic tables, and car tailgates. Enter the Coleman deluxe Camp Kitchen, a complete outdoor food preparation center that fits inside a compact carrying case.


The Camp Kitchen includes a removable dish tub for post-meal cleanup.
The Camp Kitchen is outfitted with a rust-resistant aluminum frame made using heavy-duty channel-extruded aluminum for added strength. More important than the construction, however, is the functionality. The kitchen's main body includes a moisture-resistant MDF tabletop food prep area with plenty of space for chopping onions and shaping hamburger patties. To the side is a wire-grid extender shelf that easily holds most Coleman and other brands of camping stoves at countertop level. No more cooking on the ground or an unstable stump. It's the extras that really make the Camp Kitchen go. The unit offers a handy bottom shelf for storing items like pots, dishware, and bread, along with chrome-plated wire hooks and a spice rack/shelves along the back for mugs, spatulas, and condiments. The Camp Kitchen even includes a removable tub for washing your dishes and a produce bin. And when you're ready to head home, the Camp Kitchen folds up into an easy-to-carry suitcase-style frame that fits easily in most car trunks. Other details include stable leg braces for added support, plastic guards to protect the corners, a lantern hook, a paper-towel holder, and a hand-towel holder. The Camp Kitchen measures 74.5 by 64 by 20 inches (W x H x D) when open and 39.5 by 7 by 20 inches when closed and weighs 24.2 pounds. About Coleman
More than 100 years ago, a young man with an entrepreneurial spirit and a better idea began manufacturing lanterns in Wichita, Kansas. His name was W.C. Coleman, and the company he founded would change life in America. A man plagued with such poor vision he sometimes had to ask classmates to read aloud to him, Coleman saw a brilliant light in 1900 in a drugstore window that stopped him in his tracks. He inquired about the light inside and discovered he was able to read even the small print on a medicine bottle by the illumination. The lamps had mantles, not wicks, and were fueled by gasoline under pressure instead of coal oil. Soon afterward, Coleman started a lighting service that offered a "no light, no pay" clause--a big step forward for merchants who were burned by inferior products that rarely worked--and drew substantial interest from businesses that wanted to keep their lights on after dark. In the ensuing years, Coleman expanded its product line well beyond lanterns. The company's current catalog is thick with products that make spending time outdoors a pleasure. There are coolers that keep food and drinks cold for days on end, comfortable airbeds that won't deflate during the night, a complete line of LED lights that last for years, powerful portable grills that cook with an authentic open-grill flame, and much, much more. Coleman has truly fashioned much of our outdoor camping experience, and expects to do so for generations to come.
data.reviewText: This works awesome. Its easy to tear down and assemble. No tools required. Only drawback is its a little on the heavy side. But if u want the support and durability, I guess its expected. I would definitely recommend to purchase for your camping adventures.

data.title: Coleman Folding Shovel
data.description: Dig a fire pit and cut small branches at the campsite with a Coleman Folding Shovel. This compact tool is made of durable steel with a serrated edge for sawing. It measures 10 in. (25.4 cm) when it's folded and fits inside a backpack. When you're ready to use it, the shovel folds out to 23 in. (58.42 cm) and easily locks into place for safety. During the winter, keep the shovel in your trunk to clear snow and ice in an emergency.
data.reviewText: For ten dollars these are a good camp shovel.  Strong construction and take a good bit of abuse.  For the price I can't really think of anything negative about these tools.  Recommend.

data.title: Coleman 40-Quart PowerChill Hot/Cold Thermoelectric Cooler
data.description: You choose: keep your food warm OR cool, and never need ice or a freezer pack, with the Coleman 40-Quart PowerChill Hot/Cold Cooler. The PowerChill uses electricity to keep your food at the right temperature. On Cool mode, it cools down to forty degrees below the surrounding temperature; on Warm, it heats to 110-140 degrees above the surrounding temperature. Just plug it into your car's 12-volt outlet, or purchase the 110-volt AC adapter (sold separately) for use in the home. The PowerChill 40 Quart can hold 44 cans, plus snacks. The interior tray helps keep everything organized, and you can use the PowerChill upright, cabinet-style, or horizontally, chest-style. The door can be converted to open from the left or the right, so it'll work well in your available space. 1-year limited warranty.
data.reviewText:
It actually just came in today. I have it connected to a 10 amp power supply that came with a 12v receptacle. So far it's dropping its internal temp 10 degrees per hour.
Note 1: It arrived in perfect shape, and appears to be very well built and finished. I particularly like this line in the Manual:
  "This cooler will not chill foods and beverages as fast as your home refrigerator. Please don't expect it to do so."
Note 2:  It does NOT come with an AC adapter of any kind, contrary to some of the reviews on Amazon. However, the Manual tells how to order
  one.
Note 3:  I had an earlier version of this cooler for 5 or 10 years, and might have killed it by putting ice and icy foods in it.

It actually just came in today. I have it connected to a 10 amp power supply that came with a 12v receptacle. So far it's dropping its internal temp 10 degrees per hour.
Note 1: It arrived in perfect shape, and appears to be very well built and finished. I particularly like this line in the Manual:
  "This cooler will not chill foods and beverages as fast as your home refrigerator. Please don't expect it to do so."
Note 2:  It does NOT come with an AC adapter of any kind, contrary to some of the reviews on Amazon. However, the Manual tells how to order
  one.
Note 3:  I had an earlier version of this cooler for 5 or 10 years, and might have killed it by putting ice and icy foods in it.

data.title: Coleman Propane Grill | RoadTrip LXE Portable Gas Grill
data.description: Stop manhandling those heavy, bulky backyard beasts and show off as the grill master with the Coleman RoadTrip LXE Propane Grill. It's easy to tote around and set up. Just fold it up, roll it with one hand to your favorite cooking spot and set it up in a matter of seconds with the lift and lock system. The grill comes equipped with durable cast-iron grilling surfaces. (Coleman RoadTrip pot support and griddles are available separately.) (1838.7 sq. cm) of cooking surface. It's perfect for BBQs, picnics, tailgating, camping or a night on the patio. data.title: Coleman Rainfly Accessory for 6-Person Instant Tent
data.description:
Get better airflow with the Coleman Rainfly Accessory for 6-Person Instant Tent. This rainfly for the Coleman 6-Person Instant Tent, 10 x 9, allows you to crack your windows during a downpour and still stay dry. Get better airflow when camping in your Coleman Instant six-person tent with this Instant tent rainfly. Coleman tents are well sealed and protected from the rain, so campers sometime lose airflow when it starts to pour. Designed for Coleman's 10-by-9-foot Instant tents, the rainfly not only provides added protection from the weather, but it also allows you to crack your windows during a storm and still stay dry. Plus, the rainfly creates a spacious vestibule outside--perfect for stowing shoes and other gear.

data.title: Coleman Camp Axe
data.description: Get the right tool for multiple jobs when you're at the campsite with the Coleman Camp Axe. The sharp edge of the durable drop-forged steel head easily splits wood for a campfire or stove, while the flat edge works wonders for driving tent stakes into hard ground. When it's time to pack up, pulling your tent stakes is easy with the notch cut into the head. Durable and well built, the Coleman Camp Axe is perfect for the campsite, wooded terrain, or the backyard. The axe is equipped with a drop-forged carbon-steel axe head that easily splits wood into kindling, along with a forged steel handle and an ergonomic nonslip grip. With the Coleman Camp Axe, you'll be ready for any camping contingency.

data.title: Coleman 40-Can Collapsible Soft Cooler
data.description: Have the convenience of a large cooler when you need one and extra storage room in your home when you don't. The Coleman 40 Can Collapsible Cooler is big enough to hold almost two cases of soda and then collapse down to the size of a phone book when you're ready to put it away. A bungee cord on the back, front pocket and external lid storage make keeping snacks and utensils convenient. An adjustable shoulder strap makes it easy to carry. Antimicrobial, product-protecting properties are built into the flexible lining to resist odor, mold and mildew, making it easy to clean between uses. Heat-welded seams help prevent soft liner leaks.
data.reviewText: Great lunch box for work. My only complaint is that the rectangle board that you push to the bottom to give it it's shape is absorbent. Anything that spills in the bag that piece absorbed and after a few weeks had to toss it. I still have the bag though, it's just not always box shaped.

data.title: Coleman Exponent Multi-Fuel Stove
data.description: The Coleman Multi-Fuel Stove offers sturdy, compact design and versatile, powerful performance. The integrated fuel tank operates on your choice of Coleman fuel, unleaded gasoline or kerosene (kerosene generator included). The sturdy fold-out legs keep the stove stable, while burner bowl and pot supports help protect the flame even in windy conditions. Precise flame control allows you to go from simmer to boil. Stove weighs 21.6 ounces. Get ready to cook with the Coleman Exponent 550B725 Multi-Fuel camp stove, which weighs 21.6 ounces. Fill up the integrated tank one time and use it all weekend. It operates on either Coleman fuel or unleaded gasoline, and can also operate on kerosene with an optional generator. Thanks to Coleman's PerfectFlow feature, it's regulated for a constant, consistent burn (without flaring or flickering). Other features include fold-out legs and wind-protecting burner bowel and pot supports. The Coleman Exponent 550-6771 Maintenance Kit is a recommended accessory.
data.reviewText: I wanted to like this stove while I used it for car camping.  But it has one very serious flaw.  It won't turn off and spills unburned fuel creating a massive fire hazard.  It wouldn't turn off when brand new.  So I called Coleman support who were very helpful.  We determined which part failed and they sent out a replacement, no charge.  I installed it and it ran fine for several years but recently spilled a cup of white gas on the tailgate because, again, it wouldn't turn off.  It is too dangerous to keep so I threw it in the trash.  The off switch should be a needle valve but this design is just a thin wire running through the top tube to plug the jet.  Use this at your own risk
data.text: TITLE: Coleman Exponent Multi-Fuel Stove DESCRIPTION: The Coleman Multi-Fuel Stove offers sturdy, compact design and versatile, powerful performance. The integrated fuel tank operates on your choice of Coleman fuel, unleaded gasoline or kerosene (kerosene generator included). The sturdy fold-out legs keep the stove stable, while burner bowl and pot supports help protect the flame even in windy conditions. Precise flame control allows you to go from simmer to boil. Stove weighs 21.6 ounces. Get ready to cook with the Coleman Exponent 550B725 Multi-Fuel camp stove, which weighs 21.6 ounces. Fill up the integrated tank one time and use it all weekend. It operates on either Coleman fuel or unleaded gasoline, and can also operate on kerosene with an optional generator. Thanks to Coleman's PerfectFlow feature, it's regulated for a constant, consistent burn (without flaring or flickering). Other features include fold-out legs and wind-protecting burner bowel and pot supports. The Coleman Exponent 550-6771 Maintenance Kit is a recommended accessory. REVIEW: I wanted to like this stove while I used it for car camping. But it has one very serious flaw. It won't turn off and spills unburned fuel creating a massive fire hazard. It wouldn't turn off when brand new. So I called Coleman support who were very helpful. We determined which part failed and they sent out a replacement, no charge. I installed it and it ran fine for several years but recently spilled a cup of white gas on the tailgate because, again, it wouldn't turn off. It is too dangerous to keep so I threw it in the trash. The off switch should be a needle valve but this design is just a thin wire running through the top tube to plug the jet. Use this at your own risk

data.title: Coleman Sundome Dome Tent
data.reviewText:
My boyfriend and I took this tent on a practice camping trip before our week long camping trip. It rained pretty hard that night and the tent held up well. Zippers worked, the electrical zipper is a nice addition. However the rain did leak in from that corner of the tent.

We liked that the waterproof tarping on the bottom goes a coupl inches above the bottom of the tent. We had to sleep on some water, and it didn't leak.

I think this is a pretty sturdy tent. Worth the money and I can see us using it for many more camping trips.

My boyfriend and I took this tent on a practice camping trip before our week long camping trip. It rained pretty hard that night and the tent held up well. Zippers worked, the electrical zipper is a nice addition. However the rain did leak in from that corner of the tent.

We liked that the waterproof tarping on the bottom goes a coupl inches above the bottom of the tent. We had to sleep on some water, and it didn't leak.

I think this is a pretty sturdy tent. Worth the money and I can see us using it for many more camping trips.

data.title: Coleman 16-Can Soft Cooler with Removable Liner
data.description: The Coleman 16-Can Soft Cooler goes with you to picnics, BBQs, camping, sporting events, tailgates and beyond. It holds 16 cans inside, and features plenty of extra storage space in its front zippered pocket, side mesh pockets, and mesh pocket in the lid. There are even bungees on the lid for stowing more extras! Sporting an adjustable shoulder strap, the cooler is soft on the outside for comfortable carrying, and has a hard plastic liner inside to protect your food from being crushed. The liner material is FDA approved for food contact, and is removable for easy cleaning. The Soft Cooler provides antimicrobial protection, to resist odors, mold, and mildew.
data.reviewText: I tried it during my camping trip last weekend. I did a decent job keeping things cool. But it is not a YETI or RTIC by any means. Don't expect it to keep your ice for more than a few hours. Good for day trips.
data.text:
TITLE:
Coleman 16-Can Soft Cooler with Removable Liner

DESCRIPTION:
The Coleman 16-Can Soft Cooler goes with you to picnics, BBQs, camping, sporting events, tailgates and beyond. It holds 16 cans inside, and features plenty of extra storage space in its front zippered pocket, side mesh pockets, and mesh pocket in the lid. There are even bungees on the lid for stowing more extras! Sporting an adjustable shoulder strap, the cooler is soft on the outside for comfortable carrying, and has a hard plastic liner inside to protect your food from being crushed. The liner material is FDA approved for food contact, and is removable for easy cleaning. The Soft Cooler provides antimicrobial protection, to resist odors, mold, and mildew.

REVIEW:
I tried it during my camping trip last weekend. I did a decent job keeping things cool. But it is not a YETI or RTIC by any means. Don't expect it to keep your ice for more than a few hours. Good for day trips.

data.title: Coleman 8D Square Pack-Away Full Size Lantern
data.description: Designed for convenience, the Coleman 8D Pack-Away Family Size Lantern features a clever telescoping design. Packed up, the lantern creates a built-in carry case for compact and easy storage and transport. Simply pull up the top to unpack the lantern to its full size (12" tall). The lantern's 15-watt fluorescent spiral U-tube provides an extra-bright, white light, with a reflector that directs light upward and outward for more efficient illumination -- it sheds an impressive 390 lumens of light on High. The light automatically turns off when the lantern is closed, to conserve your batteries. The lantern's bail handle is convenient for comfortable carrying or for hanging. The 8D Pack-Away Lantern runs for up to 24 hours on low or 12 hours on high, with a single set of eight D batteries (sold separately). The bulb and battery are easy to access and replace. Includes a limited 5-year warranty. Lantern measures approximately 12.13" x 6.81" x 6.57" extended, and weighs 2.58 pounds without batteries.
data.reviewText: This little portable is a neat design and stores away well when traveling.  I didn't give it a higher rating because there are other lights that require 8 batteries that give off more light and cost less.  Of course, they are LED not fluorescent, which is becoming pass.  This is a very good lighting unit but it has been surpassed with more light emitting LED models.

data.title: Coleman 8-Person Camping Tent with Built-in Closet | Tenaya Lake Cabin Tent with Fast Pitch Setup
data.description: Enjoy some comforts from home while camping with the Coleman Tenaya Lake Fast Pitch 8-Person Cabin Tent with Closet. This camping tent includes a built-in closet with shelves and a hanger bar, making it easy to keep your clothes and gear organized without having to dig through your backpack. Thanks to pre-attached, color-coded top poles and hub, fast-fit feet, and snag-free Insta-Clip suspension, this cabin tent with the Fast Pitch system assembles 55 percent quicker than similar Coleman tents with a conventional setup. The WeatherTec system with patented welded floors and inverted seams helps ensure you and your gear stay dry, while the included rainfly offers extra weather protection. This backpacking tent offers enough room for two queen air beds and comes with a room divider for added privacy and storage pockets to keep your items stored away. An E-Port lets you easily bring power inside the tent. A hinged door lets you enter and exit the tent effortlessly, and Illumiline reflective guy lines offer greater visibility at night, so you wont accidentally trip in the dark. And when its time to pack up, the 8-person tent fits neatly inside the included wheeled carry bag for easy storage and transportation.
data.reviewText: it takes at least 3 people to put it up.

data.title: Coleman Trailhead II Cot
data.reviewText:
Well - I should have believed all the comments about installing the crossbar!  The wife and I spent 30 minutes trying everything to get the 2nd crossbar in place on both cots - no go.  It is simply impossible unless you use a screwdriver or some type of wedge.  I REFUSE to use a screwdriver to put together a darn cot!  Hey, if Amazon wants to cover return postage for everyone with this problem - I guess it is in their cost model.  I for one will never buy another coleman product.  Obviously they know the problem but simply don't care.

UPDATE:  Just sent Coleman a e-mail outlining my problem (and that of others) - will let ya know what they say.
FLASH - REPLY:  HOLD ONTO YOU SEATS MY FRIENDS!  Coleman replied and said they NEVER HEARD of the crossbar problem.  I believe that says it all - unreal.

data.title: Coleman 52 qt. Xtreme Cooler
data.description: The Coleman 52 qt. Xtreme Cooler is perfect for weekend getaways, with the technology to keep ice up to five days in 90-degree weather and the capacity to hold 82 cans. It features two-way handles for easy lifting and carrying, and the rustproof, leak-resistant channel drain allows for no-tilt draining.   You know the name, and you?ll love the company. Coleman is unparalleled in the world of camping and outdoor gear. What started out as an innovation in gas-powered lamps over 100 years ago has grown to an international company that designs and constructs top-quality tents, lanterns, stoves, coolers, sleeping bags, and more. No matter what your outdoor sport: hiking, camping, fishing, hunting, boating, swimming, four-wheeling, relaxing, or tailgating, Coleman has what you need to help get you out there to enjoy it. Durable plastic construction. Features Xtreme 5 technology and Thermozone insulation. Designed to hold up to 82 cans. Includes hinged lid with 4 cup holders and 2-way handle. Dimensions: 27.75L x 14.95W x 17.25H in..

data.title: Coleman 42-Can Soft Cooler with Removable Liner & Wheels
data.description: The Coleman 42-Can Wheeled Soft Cooler goes with you to the campsite, the BBQ, sporting events, tailgates and beyond. It features durable wheels and a retractable handle, so it's easy and comfortable to transport to wherever the party is. The cooler is big enough to hold 42 cans, and features plenty of extra storage space in its front zippered pocket, side mesh pockets, and mesh pocket in the lid. There are even bungees on the lid for stowing more extras! The cooler is soft on the outside and has a hard plastic liner inside to protect the contents from being crushed. The liner material is FDA approved for food contact, and is removable for easy cleaning. The Soft Cooler provides to resist odors, mold, and mildew.
data.reviewText:
This cooler really works quite well. I bought it based on good reviews and the fact that it claimed to hold 42 standard 12oz. beverage cans. C'mon, let's be real, that means beers. Anyway, with the plastic insert installed, I can't find any way to fit more than 36 cans in this. Not a deal-breaker, but I'm not sure where the extra 6 cans are supposed to go.

The plastic liner, after just a few weeks of ownership, has already saved me a ton of hassle when cleaning up after a picnic or rooftop drinking session with friends. We've had leaky cans, not-quite-empty bottles, and trash in this cooler so far and the liner is super easy to remove and rinse off with the hose. Dealing with a dirty and wet interior of the cooler would be quite a bit more trouble. Great job, Coleman!

I appreciate the small, but useful, exterior pockets, including one that zips closed. Good for your wallet, keys, sunglasses, phone, and maybe a small bluetooth speaker. The handle is a good length for an average-height adult and the wheels, while small, seem to handle rolling through the grass just fine. A big downside to the handle is that it doesn't lock, so picking the cooler up one-handed isn't easy and you also can't lean on the handle when it's extended. This would be my one suggestion for improvement.
data.text:
TITLE:
Coleman 42-Can Soft Cooler with Removable Liner & Wheels

DESCRIPTION:
The Coleman 42-Can Wheeled Soft Cooler goes with you to the campsite, the BBQ, sporting events, tailgates and beyond. It features durable wheels and a retractable handle, so it's easy and comfortable to transport to wherever the party is. The cooler is big enough to hold 42 cans, and features plenty of extra storage space in its front zippered pocket, side mesh pockets, and mesh pocket in the lid. There are even bungees on the lid for stowing more extras! The cooler is soft on the outside and has a hard plastic liner inside to protect the contents from being crushed. The liner material is FDA approved for food contact, and is removable for easy cleaning. The Soft Cooler provides to resist odors, mold, and mildew.

REVIEW:
This cooler really works quite well. I bought it based on good reviews and the fact that it claimed to hold 42 standard 12oz. beverage cans. C'mon, let's be real, that means beers. Anyway, with the plastic insert installed, I can't find any way to fit more than 36 cans in this. Not a deal-breaker, but I'm not sure where the extra 6 cans are supposed to go.

The plastic liner, after just a few weeks of ownership, has already saved me a ton of hassle when cleaning up after a picnic or rooftop drinking session with friends. We've had leaky cans, not-quite-empty bottles, and trash in this cooler so far and the liner is super easy to remove and rinse off with the hose. Dealing with a dirty and wet interior of the cooler would be quite a bit more trouble. Great job, Coleman!

I appreciate the small, but useful, exterior pockets, including one that zips closed. Good for your wallet, keys, sunglasses, phone, and maybe a small bluetooth speaker. The handle is a good length for an average-height adult and the wheels, while small, seem to handle rolling through the grass just fine. A big downside to the handle is that it doesn't lock, so picking the cooler up one-handed isn't easy and you also can't lean on the handle when it's extended. This would be my one suggestion for improvement.

data.title: Coleman Camp Propane Grill/Stove+
data.description: The Coleman Camp Propane Grill/Stove+ offers both a grill and a stove for versatile campsite cooking. This durable steel-crafted outdoor appliance has a stove surface that fits a 10-inch pan as well as a 130-square inch grill area, letting you saut veggies while you're grilling chicken, or get the soup going while you're cooking up burgers. Each surface has independent temperature controls to give you precise control. The InstaStart push-button ignition powers up the portable grill with a simple press of a button with no matches needed, while integrated Wind Block panels help shield the flame from wind and can be folded down on calm days to be used as side tables. This outdoor grill features PerfectFlow pressure control technology to keep the heat steady even in extreme conditions and PerfectHeat technology for more efficient cooking with less fuel. An aluminized steel cooktop offers durable use and is rust resistant, and the removable grates and grease management tray offer simple and quick cleanup. The Coleman Camp Propane Grill/Stove+ can run for up to one hour with both burners on high setting on one 16.4-ounce propane cylinder (sold separately) and produces 20,000 total BTUs of cooking power.
data.reviewText: Unable to contol the flame. I wouldn't buy it again

data.title: Coleman QuickPack Deluxe Propane Lantern
data.description: Make packing up your lantern as easy as possible with the Coleman QuickPack Deluxe Propane Lantern. The 2-mantle design of this lantern creates a steady, bright glow of up to 1000 lumens of light that reaches up to 22 meters away. A quick match strike is all you need to get your lantern started, then simply dial in the perfect brightness with the adjustable control knob. Once your lantern is lit, the wide base provides stability when placed on a table, and the bail handle allows for easy hanging. No matter where you go, the PerfectFlow pressure-control technology produces a steady fuel stream and consistent performance in all kinds of weather. When you're ready to head home, the QuickPack storage system helps get you on your way quickly, since the lantern base is also the bottom of your case. Just place the outer cover and you're all packed up until you're ready for your next bright adventure.
data.reviewText: I own 3 of these lanterns, and 2 out of 3 of the valves leak slowly when shut off (and did so right out of the box) making it absolutely necessary to remove it from the tank, (or close the tank valve on a 20# tank) when not in use.
data.text:
TITLE:
Coleman QuickPack Deluxe Propane Lantern

DESCRIPTION:
Make packing up your lantern as easy as possible with the Coleman QuickPack Deluxe Propane Lantern. The 2-mantle design of this lantern creates a steady, bright glow of up to 1000 lumens of light that reaches up to 22 meters away. A quick match strike is all you need to get your lantern started, then simply dial in the perfect brightness with the adjustable control knob. Once your lantern is lit, the wide base provides stability when placed on a table, and the bail handle allows for easy hanging. No matter where you go, the PerfectFlow pressure-control technology produces a steady fuel stream and consistent performance in all kinds of weather. When you're ready to head home, the QuickPack storage system helps get you on your way quickly, since the lantern base is also the bottom of your case. Just place the outer cover and you're all packed up until you're ready for your next bright adventure.

REVIEW:
I own 3 of these lanterns, and 2 out of 3 of the valves leak slowly when shut off (and did so right out of the box) making it absolutely necessary to remove it from the tank, (or close the tank valve on a 20# tank) when not in use.

data.title: Coleman Sportsman Waterproof First Aid Kit, 100-Piece for boating
data.description: Campers, hikers, hunters, bikers, boaters, and other outdoor enthusiasts can rest easier with six first aid kit options available under the Coleman brand - the most trusted name in outdoor equipment. Each kit contains components that are most used and useful when you or your family needs medical attention away from home. Colemans Sportmans Waterproof First Aid Kit contains: 20 - Bandages 1" x 3", 20 - Small Bandages, 8 - Butterfly Bandages, 16 - Spot Bandages, 5 - Knuckle Bandages, 8 - Antiseptic Wipes, 4 - Sting relief Wipes, 2 - Large Safety Pins, 2 - Small Safety Pins, 4 - Antibiotic Ointments, 1 - Razor Blade, 2 - Rubber Gloves (1 pair), 6 - Gauze Pads 2" x 2", 1 - 1/2 x 2 1/2 Yards Adhesive Tape, 1 - Waterproof Case.
data.reviewText: The kit has plenty of essentials but the waterproof box itself is not too sturdy.  The first time I opened it, the metal clamp on one of the sides fell off.  When I inspected the item, it turned out that the plastic that holds it in place broke.  I would have expected camping gear announced for outdoor uses and waterproof, would be sturdier than that...

data.title: Coleman EasyStay Single High Airbed
data.description: Pack lightweight support with a Coleman Quickbed Lite Airbed. With its internal coils, you ll rest on superior comfort and support, while a soft top offers relaxation for sleepers who are on the go. Once you determine your perfect firmness, it will be yours all night long with the airtight system that's guaranteed not to leak and the easy to use double lock valve that also helps inflate and deflate the bed quickly. When you're ready to pack up, the built in storage system makes putting the bed away a matter of folding it, rolling it and storing it.
data.reviewText: Tried to use this over the weekend camping and within minutes of getting it aired up, it went flat! I am NOT impressed with this airbed what so ever. I can honestly say with everything I have ever ordered on Amazon this was the worst purchase ever.

data.title: Coleman SUV/Packable Twin QuickBed
data.description: Drive your "tent" up to the campsite when the Coleman SUV/Packable Twin QuickBed makes the back of your vehicle a cozy place to sleep. The airbed is the perfect size when you lay the seats down and spread out. An expansion pillow even allows you to customize your bed's size for your SUV. Once you find your perfect firmness, the comfort lasts all night long with the Airtight System that's guaranteed not to leak and the Double Lock Valve that allows the bed to deflate and inflate quickly. When you're ready to pack up, the built-in patented Wrap 'N' Roll Storage System makes putting the bed away a matter of folding it, rolling it and storing it. The bed is also great for vehicle emergency kits.

data.title: Coleman Beach Shade
data.description: " Take your own shady, private space to the beach or park with the Coleman Beach Shade. This pop-up beach tent is made with UVGuard material that offers 50+ UPF protection from the suns harmful rays and durable Polyguard material that is designed to last season after season. The extended floor can be zipped shut for privacy while changing clothes, and the dry line offers a convenient place to hang wet bathing suits and towels. Integrated storage pockets give you a secure place to put your items while you swim or sunbathe. And when it gets hot, the back window can be unzipped to let the breeze in, while the sand bag compartments can be filled as needed to anchor the beach canopy on windy days. The Coleman Beach Shade comes with ground stakes and pre-attached guy lines and can be set up in about five minutes.
data.reviewText: This is not an easy set up and really sucks for Hawaii because of the consistent wind. I thought it would be a pop-up type sunshade but it took about 15 min to set it up. Think of it like setting up a tent with 4 different inconvenient tent poles. The wind blew it away on the beach about three times so I took it down. Packed into a normal suitcase like a champ though. Good quality. Great for inside your house to pretend you are camping.
data.text:
TITLE:
Coleman Beach Shade

DESCRIPTION:
" Take your own shady, private space to the beach or park with the Coleman Beach Shade. This pop-up beach tent is made with UVGuard material that offers 50+ UPF protection from the suns harmful rays and durable Polyguard material that is designed to last season after season. The extended floor can be zipped shut for privacy while changing clothes, and the dry line offers a convenient place to hang wet bathing suits and towels. Integrated storage pockets give you a secure place to put your items while you swim or sunbathe. And when it gets hot, the back window can be unzipped to let the breeze in, while the sand bag compartments can be filled as needed to anchor the beach canopy on windy days. The Coleman Beach Shade comes with ground stakes and pre-attached guy lines and can be set up in about five minutes.

REVIEW:
This is not an easy set up and really sucks for Hawaii because of the consistent wind. I thought it would be a pop-up type sunshade but it took about 15 min to set it up. Think of it like setting up a tent with 4 different inconvenient tent poles. The wind blew it away on the beach about three times so I took it down. Packed into a normal suitcase like a champ though. Good quality. Great for inside your house to pretend you are camping.

data.title: Coleman 8-Person Tent for Camping | Red Canyon Car Camping Tent
data.description: Measuring 17- by 10-feet, Coleman's Red Canyon tent is spacious enough to comfortably sleep eight campers. Ideal for weekend camping trips with family and friends, the Red Canyon features removable privacy walls, allowing you to create up to three separate rooms.
data.reviewText: The tent itself was great.  Unfortunately the one we sent had been marked "returned,opened but never used" which would have been fine, but it was most definitely used.  The bottom was still wet and muddy and there was a dead butterfly inside when we set it up, with tears in the screens and side. It was very quick to set up (I could do it myself in about 20 minutes and it was the first tent I'd ever touched, my kids were pretty proud) but it's either easy to tear or the people who used it before were crazy.
data.text:
TITLE:
Coleman 8-Person Tent for Camping | Red Canyon Car Camping Tent

DESCRIPTION:
Measuring 17- by 10-feet, Coleman's Red Canyon tent is spacious enough to comfortably sleep eight campers. Ideal for weekend camping trips with family and friends, the Red Canyon features removable privacy walls, allowing you to create up to three separate rooms.

REVIEW:
The tent itself was great.  Unfortunately the one we sent had been marked "returned,opened but never used" which would have been fine, but it was most definitely used.  The bottom was still wet and muddy and there was a dead butterfly inside when we set it up, with tears in the screens and side. It was very quick to set up (I could do it myself in about 20 minutes and it was the first tent I'd ever touched, my kids were pretty proud) but it's either easy to tear or the people who used it before were crazy.

data.title: Coleman BlackCat PerfecTemp Catalytic Heater with InstaStart Technology
data.description:
A great portable heater to take with you on a camping trip, to a sports outing or in your golf cart, the Coleman BlackCat InstaStart Heater features a 3000-BTU heater that will run for up to 7 hours on a single 16.4-ounce can of propane (sold separately). The BlackCat features PerfecTemp Catalytic technology, for safe, efficient, flameless heat, while Coleman's InstaStart battery ignition provides push-button, matchless lighting. A convenient switch offers both High and Low temperature settings for more control. The BlackCat Heater's legs fold up for easy storage, and an easy-grip handle provides comfortable, convenient carrying. Limited 5-year warranty. The Coleman 5038-850 BlackCat Perfectemp Catalytic Propane Heater with InstaStart Technology provides you with comfortable warmth indoors or in a tent. This catalytic heater has an electronic ignition which is not only quicker and easier that using a match, but is much more reliable in cold, windy weather. This Coleman BlackCat heater is incredibly portable for its heat output, with an easy-to-grip handle that makes it easy to carry, and compact legs that integrate into its collar for easy storage. The BlackCat's 3,000 BTU output will operate for up to seven hours on one 16.4 ounce cylinder of propane (not included). Manufacturer Warranty
Limited lifetime warranty
data.reviewText:
I bought this because we were going camping while it was still pretty cold outside.  When you get it you need to burn it to get rid of the initial fumes.  It lights easily and does project a warm area in front of the unit.  If the air is still you can use it outside.  If it is windy forget about it.  It is still not clear if it is recommended for use inside a tent while sleeping.  I found conflicting information.  We have a large 16X8 dome tent with an open mesh top covered by a fly so I felt like it would be OK to run inside.  I'm still alive so it didn't kill me.  It did warm the tent but in reality the heat is going out the top of the tent.  It helped keep us warm during a very cold night. We actually got some sleep; everyone else in our group was groggy the next day because they were so cold. I woke up at 2 am with a frozen face and replaced they empty cylinder then relit it.

There are heaters that burn outside the tent and warm inside but they are big and expensive.

My wife and kids liked the comfort.  Is it worth the cost of the unit and a few cylinders of gas to keep you warm at night?  For us the answer is yes in the right situation.  When it's cold and you have kids or a frozen wife it's worth its weight in gold.

The quality is decent, not great so I give it 4 stars.

data.title: Coleman Hooligan Backpacking Tent
data.description: The Hooligan 2 from Coleman is a 2 person backpacking tent. It features a full coverage coated polyester fly (450mm) that envelops the tent in a cocoon of protection from the harshest weather. A small front vestibule offers some storage space for extra gear and the all mesh body is great for ventilation and stargazing on nights when the weather is clear. The fly seams are sealed at the factory and the rugged 1000D polyethylene floor is a bathtub style (wraps up the sides) for extreme weather protection and has welded seams, even in the corners, to guard against leaks. Set up is with a single pole and thus could not be more simple and quick. Great for beginner backpackers, scouts and those who want to keep it simple.Non Freestanding (1 pole) design is simple to set up and take down.All mesh body for maximum ventilation and comfort and reduces condensation.Zippered back vent in the fly helps to keep the tent comfortable - adjustable through zipper on the back tent wall.Sturdy 11mm (1ea) Shockcorded fiberglass frame for easy set up.All mesh body is perfect for stargazing on nice nights.Full coverage fly is perfect for harsh weather.1 Small storage vestibule - 6 sq. ft. - good for boots etc..1 interior storage pocket.Easy convenient continuous pole sleeve construction.Pin and ring pole to body connection is fast and simple.External guy points to keep the tent stable in storms and high winds.Instructions sewn to carry bag so they are easy to find and hard to lose.All carry sacks and stakes included.Includes convenient flashlight loop on the ceiling.Fire retardant.Import..
data.reviewText:
Firstly, for the price this tent is worth it ($70 on amazon prime). I took this tent into a moderately difficult portaging/canoeing trip into the bwca in MN. It was perfect for the trip. Not too heavy and the packed shape isn't too cumbersome given the size and features. I was pleased.  The best feature of this tent is the expansive vestibule of the rainfly. It features a main one near the front and small ones along the other three sides. Helped a ton during some pretty nasty thunderstorms.  Combine it with a heavy duty tarp for the base and you and your gear will be bone dry.

Now for some suggestions:
The main criticism is that the way the tent is pitched has changed a bit and the instructions need to be updated.  The rod for the vestibule is no longer guided through sleeves but through velcro straps.  The same type of straps are used to secure the rest of the rainfly to the main tent rods. Practice before you take this tent out! It will not be intuitively obvious which straps go to which rod.

Second criticism: There is no loop at the top of the tent interior to hang a lantern/light source. This just seems like a missed product oppurtunity but friends have informed me that I could sew a loop on. Not sure if i'm willing to risk the integrity of the tent mesh though.

data.title: Coleman Cast Iron Skillet
data.description: Grill up burgers, steaks and chicken on your next outdoor adventure with a Coleman Cast Iron Skillet. You'll have plenty of room to cook on the easy-to-clean, non-stick, 10-in,-diameter cooking surface. Raised ribs ensure an even temperature throughout, and the pour spouts makes for easy draining when you're done. A hole in the molded handle makes storing at home or at the campsite simple.
data.reviewText: Used for high-temp pan pizza.  Good weight, easy to handle, easy to clean if cleaned according to insturctions.  Good price.

data.title: Coleman CPX 4.5 LED Tent Light
data.description: The CPX 4.5 LED Tent Light by Coleman is compatible with any tent and even attaches to the tent wall allowing you to pivot the light where you need it. The CPX system lets you choose your power: three AAA cell batteries (included) or one CPX 4.5 rechargeable power cartridge (sold separately - Campmor item #84089). The battery meter changes from yellow to red as battery life is depleted.Magnetically attaches to any tent or metal surface.No tools required.Water resistant.Lifetime of light - LEDs don't need to be replaced.LED battery meter, changes color when battery power is low.Three light modes, white light on high or low and a low power amber nightlight.Size: W 2.75", H 5".Weight: 0.59 oz..
data.reviewText: This light worked well for only a few camping trips. Suddenly the device quits working for an unknown reason. We tried just about everything to get it going again with no success. Into the trash it went.

data.title: Coleman 24-Piece Enamel Dinnerware Set, Red
data.description:
Serve up your favorite foods around the campfire or portable stove with the Coleman 24-Piece Enamel Dinnerware Set. Made of double-fired enamel and featuring a timeless red speckled design, this outdoor dinnerware set offers classic style and durability and is dishwasher safe for easy cleaning. This 24-piece set includes four 10-inch dinner plates, four 12-ounce coffee mugs with handles, and four 6-inch bowls, making it a great choice for family meals at picnics, BBQs, and campsites. It also includes a silverware kit with four plastic-handled forks, spoons, and knives stored in a convenient roll-up pouch for easy organization and storage. Ideal for camping, picnicking, and even backyard use, this set of Coleman speckled dinnerware includes everything you need to eat a meal under the stars. The 24-piece set is outfitted with four 10-inch dinner plates, four 10-ounce mugs, and four 6-inch bowls, giving you and three co-campers enough containers for a main course, a side salad, and an after-dinner hot chocolate. In addition, the set comes with four knives, four spoons, and four forks, so you don't need to rely on plastic silverware when eating. Best of all, the set is durable and dishwasher-safe, with a red-speckled enamel surface that holds up to heavy use. The silverware also includes a compact carrying case to keep the cutlery bunched together. About Coleman
The Coleman Company has been creating and innovating products for recreational outdoor use since W.C. Coleman started selling gasoline-powered lanterns in 1900. Inventor of the hugely popular fold-up camp stove, Coleman developed a plastic liner for his galvanized steel coolers in 1957--the birth of the modern cooler--and the company has been improving their utility and design ever since. The array of products that bear the Coleman name now includes just about everything you might need to work or play outdoors, from tents and sleeping bags to boats, backpacks, and furniture.
data.reviewText: As other reviewers have stated, dishes have serious paint chips or paint glops.  No quality control with this product.  Returned

data.title: Coleman Industrial 30mm PVC Raincoat
data.description: Stay protected in the elements with the Coleman .30mm PVC Rain Coat. It-Inchs durably constructed of strong PVC-coated polyester to keep you dry. The Rain Coat features a center front zipper with storm flap to keep moisture out. The corduroy collar keeps your neck warm, while adjustable snap cuffs keep your arms dry. Pull the drawstring hood snugly around your face, wear it loose, or remove it altogether -- the choice is yours. There are 2 lower pockets for convenience. The Rain Coat stores compactly in the included carry bag.
data.reviewText:
This raincoat ripped around both arms on the first wear. I duct taped it and it held up for about another 20 wears. That's why I gave it 2 stars. Will not buy again.

Also, I'm 6'1" 190lbs and I got a XL. The raincoat was kinda baggy. I probably could fit into a L, but that would put more stress on the arm joints. Also the jacket went down about 2-3 inches pass my knees.

data.title: Coleman 5-Gallon Beverage Cooler
data.description: Keep the group cool and hydrated on sweltering days with a Coleman 5 Gallon Beverage Cooler. The side handles make carrying easy, and the screw-top lid stays secure during transport or while lifting this beverage cooler on a table top or bench. Once you're set up at your destination, the entire group can fill their cups from the easy-use faucet and stay refreshed all day long.
data.reviewText: Like the screw top lid.  Not as thrilled with the flimsy handles though.  Our Scout troop will be using these a lot and have concern the handles won't hold up like the older hard material.
data.text:

data.title: Coleman Jug With Water Carrier, 5 Gallons, Blue
data.description: Coleman, 5 gallon, blue, poly Lite water carrier, molded in handle & hand grip, fast flow spigot stores on the handle when not in use, non-insulated, polyethylene case resists scratches & dents. Comfortably port water to your campsite, picnic, boat, and beyond with the Coleman 5-gallon water carrier. The carrier features a tough polyethylene construction that's scratch- and impact-resistant, helping it hold up to rough handling. More importantly, the carrier's fast-flow spigot delivers instant water access; no more struggling to tip the carrier each time you fill up your water bottle. The carrier also includes a built-in molded handle and handgrip for comfortable, easy carrying.

data.title: Coleman Exponent Multi-Fuel Stove
data.description: The Coleman Multi-Fuel Stove offers sturdy, compact design and versatile, powerful performance. The integrated fuel tank operates on your choice of Coleman fuel, unleaded gasoline or kerosene (kerosene generator included). The sturdy fold-out legs keep the stove stable, while burner bowl and pot supports help protect the flame even in windy conditions. Precise flame control allows you to go from simmer to boil. Stove weighs 21.6 ounces. Get ready to cook with the Coleman Exponent 550B725 Multi-Fuel camp stove, which weighs 21.6 ounces. Fill up the integrated tank one time and use it all weekend. It operates on either Coleman fuel or unleaded gasoline, and can also operate on kerosene with an optional generator. Thanks to Coleman's PerfectFlow feature, it's regulated for a constant, consistent burn (without flaring or flickering). Other features include fold-out legs and wind-protecting burner bowel and pot supports. The Coleman Exponent 550-6771 Maintenance Kit is a recommended accessory.
data.reviewText:
I was torn between this stove and the MSR Whisperlite international.  I went with this one because it was cheaper, others recommended it and it seemed to have a better design than the other stove with less parts to get gunked up with dirt or grime or whatever else you run into while backpacking.  I like how stable the unit is.  There is this nifty elevated ring you can see in the picture.  You can move it around to level the legs better when you have it sitting on a rock or whatever.  It does have a stupid "hi/lo" sticker instead of painted or engraved markings but if you can't figure where high and where low are after using it once then should you really be out backpacking?

My first use went flawlessly.  It burned with large flames for the first 20 seconds until it was primed then it calmed down.  It burns with a very wide, blue and hot flame even on low.  This will prevent common issue with scorching as seen in the pocket rocket stoves.  The adjustment knob is located in a silly place right under the burner bowl.  As long as you aren't completely inept it should not be a problem though.  The fuel tank did get hot to the touch when on "high" which made me a bit nervous but one rarely cooks on high and the cool mountain air should keep it cool enough.

I fashioned a nice little pack box from the cardboard it came in using masking and packaging tape.  It doesn't fully enclose the stove but it will protect the burner and the valve/knobs from getting broken or bent while in your pack.

4/5 stars because its a little chunky but I expect it to perform better than any other stove.

data.title: Coleman 3000002009 28Qt Xtreme
data.description: Take the new, stylish performance of a Coleman 28 Quart extreme 3 cooler with you to the party-at the tailgate, BBQ or campsite. While your friends revel in the sleek new look, you'll enjoy the added improvements that better fit your lifestyle. The redesigned 28-quart cooler is tall enough to hold 2-liter bottles upright and large enough to hold up to 36 cans. Two molded cup holders help prevent spills, and the Have-A-Seat lid supports up to 250 lbs. (113.4 kg) so you'll always have a place to sit and rest. Inside the cooler's walls, extra Thermo zone insulation and extreme technology holds the cold for three days in temperatures up to 90 F (32.22 degree C), and still performs amazingly on hotter days. The foam is also free from CFCs, HFCs and HCFCs, which deplete the ozone. When the fun is done, the textured EZ-Clean top makes washing your cooler simple. Holds 36 cans-more than a case of soda fits inside a 28-quart cooler new, stylish outer Casing. Two molded cup holders keep drinks close. Have-a-seat lid supports up to 250 lbs. (113.4 kg) for a place to sit and rest retextured EZ-Clean top for quick, easy washing. Extreme 3 technology keeps ice up to 3 days in temperatures up to 90 degree F (32.22 degree C). internal dims : 15.0 x 9.8 x 12.1. External dims : 18.3 x 12.8 x 15.6. Weight (lbs) : 6.
data.reviewText: Will not hold ice over 8 hours at best. Disappointed with purchase.

data.title: Coleman Camping Instant Signal Mountain Tent
data.reviewText: We loved this tent for its size, ease of setting up, and overall quality - until...the second time we went to use it a poll snapped rendering the whole tent garbage. The polls are a lightweight plastic. I wish I could recommend this tent, because we otherwise liked it - but Coleman shouldn't have skimped on quality here.

data.reviewText: Good table, but legs won't fit under side if seated at a chair. Okay for camping as it is lightweight, and appropriate if you want something to elevate food or cook on. Not suitable as a portable seated table, unfortunately. For that purpose, I prefer a camp roll up table.

data.title: Coleman Hooligan Backpacking Tent
data.description: The Hooligan 2 from Coleman is a 2 person backpacking tent. It features a full coverage coated polyester fly (450mm) that envelops the tent in a cocoon of protection from the harshest weather. A small front vestibule offers some storage space for extra gear and the all mesh body is great for ventilation and stargazing on nights when the weather is clear. The fly seams are sealed at the factory and the rugged 1000D polyethylene floor is a bathtub style (wraps up the sides) for extreme weather protection and has welded seams, even in the corners, to guard against leaks. Set up is with a single pole and thus could not be more simple and quick. Great for beginner backpackers, scouts and those who want to keep it simple.Non Freestanding (1 pole) design is simple to set up and take down.All mesh body for maximum ventilation and comfort and reduces condensation.Zippered back vent in the fly helps to keep the tent comfortable - adjustable through zipper on the back tent wall.Sturdy 11mm (1ea) Shockcorded fiberglass frame for easy set up.All mesh body is perfect for stargazing on nice nights.Full coverage fly is perfect for harsh weather.1 Small storage vestibule - 6 sq. ft. - good for boots etc..1 interior storage pocket.Easy convenient continuous pole sleeve construction.Pin and ring pole to body connection is fast and simple.External guy points to keep the tent stable in storms and high winds.Instructions sewn to carry bag so they are easy to find and hard to lose.All carry sacks and stakes included.Includes convenient flashlight loop on the ceiling.Fire retardant.Import..
data.reviewText: Finally got to use my tent. I bought it then got really sick before I could use it. I was excited. Where I go on the Washington coast can be really windy. (This past trip, winds reach up near 40+MPH). It held up great, better than I expected in the wind. Where it failed was when the rain hit. It wasn't as near windy when the rain hit but it poured a solid 20+ hours straight. I was impressed till morning when the leaks started. One was on the door seam. Not the fly but on the double stitched seam. The other was in the top of the reason fly dripping down over my sleeping bag. At 37F that was cold. It did hold up to the wind with that great long rain fly but the wind would still sneak underneath and flow through. I didn't feel like the tent was going to take off with me on it like my last tent but it still leaked. I'm passed my time to return it. I'm sad that it didn't hold up. I feel like I did my research but in the end maybe I got a lemon? I would try again if I could return it but I'm past the time. Sad.

Coleman Hooligan Backpacking Tent

DESCRIPTION:
The Hooligan 2 from Coleman is a 2 person backpacking tent. It features a full coverage coated polyester fly (450mm) that envelops the tent in a cocoon of protection from the harshest weather. A small front vestibule offers some storage space for extra gear and the all mesh body is great for ventilation and stargazing on nights when the weather is clear. The fly seams are sealed at the factory and the rugged 1000D polyethylene floor is a bathtub style (wraps up the sides) for extreme weather protection and has welded seams, even in the corners, to guard against leaks. Set up is with a single pole and thus could not be more simple and quick. Great for beginner backpackers, scouts and those who want to keep it simple.Non Freestanding (1 pole) design is simple to set up and take down.All mesh body for maximum ventilation and comfort and reduces condensation.Zippered back vent in the fly helps to keep the tent comfortable - adjustable through zipper on the back tent wall.Sturdy 11mm (1ea) Shockcorded fiberglass frame for easy set up.All mesh body is perfect for stargazing on nice nights.Full coverage fly is perfect for harsh weather.1 Small storage vestibule - 6 sq. ft. - good for boots etc..1 interior storage pocket.Easy convenient continuous pole sleeve construction.Pin and ring pole to body connection is fast and simple.External guy points to keep the tent stable in storms and high winds.Instructions sewn to carry bag so they are easy to find and hard to lose.All carry sacks and stakes included.Includes convenient flashlight loop on the ceiling.Fire retardant.Import..

REVIEW:
Finally got to use my tent. I bought it then got really sick before I could use it. I was excited. Where I go on the Washington coast can be really windy. (This past trip, winds reach up near 40+MPH). It held up great, better than I expected in the wind. Where it failed was when the rain hit. It wasn't as near windy when the rain hit but it poured a solid 20+ hours straight. I was impressed till morning when the leaks started. One was on the door seam. Not the fly but on the double stitched seam. The other was in the top of the reason fly dripping down over my sleeping bag. At 37F that was cold. It did hold up to the wind with that great long rain fly but the wind would still sneak underneath and flow through. I didn't feel like the tent was going to take off with me on it like my last tent but it still leaked. I'm passed my time to return it. I'm sad that it didn't hold up. I feel like I did my research but in the end maybe I got a lemon? I would try again if I could return it but I'm past the time. Sad.

data.title: Coleman Tent Kit
data.description: Set up the tent quickly with the steel tent pegs and rubber mallet. Keep it clean for the entire campout with the nesting hand broom and dustpan. When the fun is done, use the tent stake puller to get the pegs out of the ground easily. All four of these important tools fit nicely inside the mesh carry bag for the next adventure.
data.reviewText:
Rubber mallet, cheaply made in contrast to what you might find at a hardware store but it does the job.
Stakes; sharp, plastic hooks have survived their first camping trip, not enough of them.
Dust Pan; too small for the tent size. . . . my bad
Hand Broom; too small for the tent size.
Overall, for the price you are getting what you pay for.  I will likely just go to the hardware store for a regular size dust pan and hand broom.
Best part of the deal is the mesh bag. . . . . .which you can also get at the hardware store.
data.text:
TITLE:
Coleman Tent Kit

DESCRIPTION:
Set up the tent quickly with the steel tent pegs and rubber mallet. Keep it clean for the entire campout with the nesting hand broom and dustpan. When the fun is done, use the tent stake puller to get the pegs out of the ground easily. All four of these important tools fit nicely inside the mesh carry bag for the next adventure.

REVIEW:
Rubber mallet, cheaply made in contrast to what you might find at a hardware store but it does the job.
Stakes; sharp, plastic hooks have survived their first camping trip, not enough of them.
Dust Pan; too small for the tent size. . . . my bad
Hand Broom; too small for the tent size.
Overall, for the price you are getting what you pay for.  I will likely just go to the hardware store for a regular size dust pan and hand broom.
Best part of the deal is the mesh bag. . . . . .which you can also get at the hardware store.

data.title: Coleman Camp Stove Carry Case, Small
data.description: Take your Coleman stove wherever outdoor adventures lead you with the Coleman Camp Stove Carry Case, Small. Designed to fit small Coleman 2-burner stoves measuring up to 20.4 x 12 x 4.5 inches, this rugged carry case made from PVC-coated polyester protects your stove during transportation and storage. Strong web handles provide comfort when toting stoves between campsites, while the durable zipper is designed to keep stoves safe and dry. Two propane pouches with drawstrings securely carry extra fuel canisters (sold separately).
data.reviewText: This is ok.  My husband got it as a gift and was disappointed it wasn't leather. It fits the Coleman Triton Series InstaStart 2-burner stove just fine and has great pockets for the propane.  It does the job protecting the stove and providing easy carry with everything you need in the bag.  It's ok for the money and does the job.

data.title: Coleman 9-Can Soft Cooler with Removable Liner
data.description: Keep food and drinks cold with the Coleman Soft Cooler. This compact, portable cooler bag holds up to nine cans and offers plenty of space for a family picnic or game-day snacks. Heat-welded seams in the zippered main compartment prevent leaks and maintain a cold environment, while the removable hard plastic liner fits snugly inside the soft cooler and allows you to easily dump out any ice melt. When not using the hard plastic liner, you can use ice substitutes to keep contents cold and dry. The cooler bag's easy-clean interior has an antimicrobial lining to resist formation of mold, mildew, fungus, and odor. A zippered front pocket, pocket under the lid, and two side mesh pockets make it easy to carry water bottles, snacks, silverware, and other accessories. You can also secure items to the top with the convenient bungee cords built into the lid. An adjustable shoulder strap lets you comfortably carry the cooler anywhere you go.
data.reviewText: Decent capacity, but not as insulating as I would have thought. Foam walls could be thicker.

data.title: Coleman Camp Axe
data.description: Get the right tool for multiple jobs when you're at the campsite with the Coleman Camp Axe. The sharp edge of the durable drop-forged steel head easily splits wood for a campfire or stove, while the flat edge works wonders for driving tent stakes into hard ground. When it's time to pack up, pulling your tent stakes is easy with the notch cut into the head. Durable and well built, the Coleman Camp Axe is perfect for the campsite, wooded terrain, or the backyard. The axe is equipped with a drop-forged carbon-steel axe head that easily splits wood into kindling, along with a forged steel handle and an ergonomic nonslip grip. With the Coleman Camp Axe, you'll be ready for any camping contingency.
data.reviewText: Extremely dull, the head is very small. Had to grind it down forever to get it sharp enough to cut anything. It's extremely heavy for as small as it is. I was very disappointed all around. Spend a little more and get an axe that doesn't suck

data.title: Coleman Pack-Away Camping Cot
data.description: Bring along a perfect relaxation spot on your next camping trip with the Coleman Pack-Away Camping Cot. Ideal for sitting, reclining, and sleeping, this versatile camping cot features an extra-wide design that provides ample resting room. It also comes equipped with a convenient removable side table with a cup holder, where you can easily keep drinks, magazines, and other personal items. The steel frame can support up to 300 pounds and is built to withstand season after season of rugged use. Thanks to its folding, portable design and included carry bag, this camping cot fits inside most car trunks and is easy to transport. The Coleman Pack-Away Camping Cot accommodates most people up to 6 feet 6 inches tall.
data.reviewText: It's very comfortable and makes tent camping great. The reason I didn't score it higher is because folding the legs is not the easiest. The other minor issue is when completely open, the legs tend to collapse relatively easy when no wait is applied.

data.title: Coleman PowerPack Propane Stove, Single Burner
data.description:
A handy cooking companion for camping and outdoor adventures, the Coleman PowerPack Propane Stove, Single Burner, combines performance with an ultra-portable design. This camping stove fits a large 12-inch pan on its steady base and pot support system. The portable stove comes equipped with an adjustable burner that lets you precisely control the cooking temperature and lasts up to 3 hours on high on one 16.4 oz. propane cylinder (sold separately). PerfectFlow pressure-control technology delivers steady heat output while cooking, and PerfectHeat technology promotes efficient fuel usage. When mealtime is over, the durable chrome-plated grate is removable for easy cleaning. Perfect for camping, backpacking, and other outdoor occasions, this compact camp stove is designed to produce up to 7,500 total BTUs of cooking power from a single 3-inch burner, giving you the ability to prepare a wide range of hot foods at the campsite. The Coleman 5453A700 Powerpack Single-burner Low-profile Propane Stove is pretty much as easy as it gets. The Powerpack's fully adjustable flame lets you boil water or simmer chili, while the PerfectFlow system of pressure-regulated propane provides consistent cooking performance (without flaring or flickering) by producing a steady fuel stream, even in cold weather, high altitudes, or when fuel is low. This Coleman stove is constructed of an aluminized-steel cooktop and removable, nickel-chrome grates so you spend your time on the trail instead of on cleanup duty. The large 3.25-inch, 7,500 BTU burner is to the side of the propane cylinder in a low-profile fashion that ensures this burner will always be one of Coleman's easiest and best. Manufacturer Warranty
Limited lifetime warranty
data.reviewText: Works as describe and useful.
