data.title: Coleman Xtreme 4 Marine Wheeled Cooler, 40 Quart
data.description: Take the new, stylish performance of a Coleman 40 Quart Xtreme 4 Marine Wheeled Cooler with you on the boat. While your friends revel in the sleek new look, you'll enjoy the added improvements that better fit your lifestyle. The redesigned 40-quart cooler is tall enough to hold 2-liter bottles upright and large enough to hold up to 60 cans. Four molded cup holders help prevent spills, and the Have-A-Seat Lid supports up to 250 lbs. (113.4 kg) so you'll always have a place to sit and rest. Heavy-duty wheels offer all-terrain transports and a long telescoping handle makes pulling the cooler more comfortable. Inside the cooler's walls, extra Thermo ZONE Insulation and Xtreme Technology holds the cold for four days in temperatures up to 90 F (32.22 C), and still performs amazingly on hotter days. The foam is also free from CFCs, HFCs and HCFCs, which deplete the ozone, and the outside is treated with a UV color additive that resists yellowing and weakening in the case. When the fun is done, the retextured EZ-Clean top makes washing your cooler on wheels simple.
data.reviewText:
Awsome cooler, works well for pier fishing on 900ft pier near my home.  Managed to stuff 31" Snook in it,
The handle is designed for pulling, like a luggage cart, after dozens of trips and many miles it still holds up.

data.title: Coleman Folding Shovel
data.description: Dig a fire pit and cut small branches at the campsite with a Coleman Folding Shovel. This compact tool is made of durable steel with a serrated edge for sawing. It measures 10 in. (25.4 cm) when it's folded and fits inside a backpack. When you're ready to use it, the shovel folds out to 23 in. (58.42 cm) and easily locks into place for safety. During the winter, keep the shovel in your trunk to clear snow and ice in an emergency.
data.reviewText: Poorly made shovel. The locking collar ring bends/crushes as soon as any pressure from the shovel blade is applied.  I had to buy a 1.5" heavy galvanized washer from home depot and dremmel open the center to replace the crushed collar ring. Just Remove the shovel blade and slip on the New collar end/ring.  After that it worked fine. Too bad coleman didn't spend the extra 20 cents to make the shovel more durable. If you can fabricate the collar ring yourself, then it's a good value and performed as good as the military Ames ones.

data.title: Coleman CPX 6 Rechargeable Power Cartridge
data.description: Power all your Coleman CPX 6 gear with the Coleman CPX 6 Rechargeable Power Cartridge. This rechargeable battery kit allows you to make any Coleman CPX 6 product rechargeable, helping you save money on disposable batteries and minimize waste in landfills. Each kit includes a rechargeable battery, a 12-volt adapter for convenient vehicle recharging, and a 120-volt adapter for use with conventional AC outlets. Built to resist impacts and water, the battery features a rugged design and comes fitted with a rubber plug to protect against dirt and debris. It recharges quickly via either a vehicle or home outlet. And thanks to the power cartridge's charge indicator, you'll know exactly when the battery is fully charged.
data.reviewText: The charge does not last as long as I would like.  It is great for a weekend or over-night trip.  I need a device for a week in the Rockies, so I don't think this will suffice.  I found a solar device that may do the trick.  I'll let you know.

data.title: Coleman Road Trip Cast-Iron Griddle
data.description:
Coleman Roadtrip Cast Iron Accessory Griddle sits on top of a propane burner for easy cooking! Expand your camp fare menu with this Cast Iron Accessory Griddle from Coleman! Perfect for pancakes, scrambled eggs and MUCH more! Made with cast iron for even heat distribution on the entire surface. Measures 12 x 12". Get yours, right now! Coleman Roadtrip Cast Iron Accessory Griddle Turn your Coleman Road Trip grill or campfire stove grate into a portable diner with this Coleman cast-iron griddle. Made of heavy-duty, porcelain-coated cast iron, the griddle is easy to clean and long lasting. More importantly, the griddle opens up a world of menu options for your camping trip, including pancakes, fried eggs, bacon, omelets, grilled cheese sandwiches, and more. The griddle--which is compatible with Coleman 9941 and 9949 Road Trip grills--measures approximately 12 by 12 inches and weighs 7 pounds. The griddle is not compatible with Coleman's 9928 Road Trip Pro Series grills. About Coleman
More than 100 years ago, a young man with an entrepreneurial spirit and a better idea began manufacturing lanterns in Wichita, Kansas. His name was W.C. Coleman, and the company he founded would change life in America. A man plagued with such poor vision he sometimes had to ask classmates to read aloud to him, Coleman saw a brilliant light in 1900 in a drugstore window that stopped him in his tracks. He inquired about the light inside and discovered he was able to read even the small print on a medicine bottle by the illumination. The lamps had mantles, not wicks, and were fueled by gasoline under pressure instead of coal oil. Soon afterward, Coleman started a lighting service that offered a "no light, no pay" clause--a big step forward for merchants who were burned by inferior products that rarely worked--and drew substantial interest from businesses that wanted to keep their lights on after dark. In the ensuing years, Coleman expanded its product line well beyond lanterns. The company's current catalog is thick with products that make spending time outdoors a pleasure. There are coolers that keep food and drinks cold for days on end, comfortable airbeds that won't deflate during the night, a complete line of LED lights that last for years, powerful portable grills that cook with an authentic open-grill flame, and much, much more. Coleman has truly fashioned much of our outdoor camping experience, and expects to do so for generations to come.
data.reviewText: We will be having pancakes at our next tailgate this is well built and fits great the price can't be beat

data.title: Coleman RoadTrip Rolling Grill Case for LX Series Grills
data.description: Add even more portability to your Coleman RoadTrip LX Series Grill when you pack it inside a Coleman RoadTrip Rolling Case. This heavy-duty, weather-resistant case includes easy-glide wheels for more mobility and top and side handles for better control. A durable zipper and inner straps secure the grill the entire trip, and inside pockets fit accessories and condiments for whatever meal youre planning to cook up.
data.reviewText:
I chose this because I thought it would be convenient and quality.  I liked the idea of a case that would carry the grill and all the accessories - grill grates, tank adapter, etc.  Unfortunately, the quality is lacking and there's not a good way to stow the handle as far as I can tell.

The very first time I went to put the grill into the case, I noticed first it wouldn't fit.  Ok, so you have to remove the handle - no big deal right?  But there's no place to put the handle - it doesn't fit will in the external mesh pocket, which is better suited for the 20 lb tank adaper hose.  Not a huge deal and I could deal with that.

Unfortunately when I went to strap in the grill inside the case using the supplied straps, one of them broke.  I was lightly cinching down the lower velcro strap and it simply pulled out - came unstitched from the case, rendering it useless.  Not what I expected from what should be a heavy duty case.  Not worth the effort of sending it back, but this shouldn't happen.
data.text:
TITLE:
Coleman RoadTrip Rolling Grill Case for LX Series Grills

DESCRIPTION:
Add even more portability to your Coleman RoadTrip LX Series Grill when you pack it inside a Coleman RoadTrip Rolling Case. This heavy-duty, weather-resistant case includes easy-glide wheels for more mobility and top and side handles for better control. A durable zipper and inner straps secure the grill the entire trip, and inside pockets fit accessories and condiments for whatever meal youre planning to cook up.

REVIEW:
I chose this because I thought it would be convenient and quality.  I liked the idea of a case that would carry the grill and all the accessories - grill grates, tank adapter, etc.  Unfortunately, the quality is lacking and there's not a good way to stow the handle as far as I can tell.

The very first time I went to put the grill into the case, I noticed first it wouldn't fit.  Ok, so you have to remove the handle - no big deal right?  But there's no place to put the handle - it doesn't fit will in the external mesh pocket, which is better suited for the 20 lb tank adaper hose.  Not a huge deal and I could deal with that.

Unfortunately when I went to strap in the grill inside the case using the supplied straps, one of them broke.  I was lightly cinching down the lower velcro strap and it simply pulled out - came unstitched from the case, rendering it useless.  Not what I expected from what should be a heavy duty case.  Not worth the effort of sending it back, but this shouldn't happen.

data.title: Coleman Jug With Water Carrier, 5 Gallons, Blue
data.description: Coleman, 5 gallon, blue, poly Lite water carrier, molded in handle & hand grip, fast flow spigot stores on the handle when not in use, non-insulated, polyethylene case resists scratches & dents. Comfortably port water to your campsite, picnic, boat, and beyond with the Coleman 5-gallon water carrier. The carrier features a tough polyethylene construction that's scratch- and impact-resistant, helping it hold up to rough handling. More importantly, the carrier's fast-flow spigot delivers instant water access; no more struggling to tip the carrier each time you fill up your water bottle. The carrier also includes a built-in molded handle and handgrip for comfortable, easy carrying.
data.reviewText: The lid did not fit right so it just leaked all over the place. Poor quality control. I feel cheated.

data.title: Coleman Propane Grill | RoadTrip LXE Portable Gas Grill
data.description: Stop manhandling those heavy, bulky backyard beasts and show off as the grill master with the Coleman RoadTrip LXE Propane Grill. It's easy to tote around and set up. Just fold it up, roll it with one hand to your favorite cooking spot and set it up in a matter of seconds with the lift and lock system. The grill comes equipped with durable cast-iron grilling surfaces. (Coleman RoadTrip pot support and griddles are available separately.) When you're ready to grill, push a button to ignite the InstaStart matchless lighting system. Cook for more than an hour on one propane cylinder with 20,000 BTUs of power on 285 sq. in. (1838.7 sq. cm) of cooking surface. It's perfect for BBQs, picnics, tailgating, camping or a night on the patio. When you're ready to leave, it folds down small enough to fit in the back of most car trunks.
data.reviewText: Really hard to regulate temperature. Have to be careful or everything burns.

data.title: Coleman Camping Instant Signal Mountain Tent
data.reviewText:
Just an absolute disaster.  First off, the directions (which are basically non-existent) make it look SO easy; which it would be if Coleman had installed the brackets and pieces right. But they didn't.  We have been fighting with this stupid tent for over 3 hours and finally gave up in spite of reading the reviews of what is wrong with it (basically, brackets are upside down, our circular piece was even upside down, and a pole that is supposed to extend doesn't extend on the top part... very frustrating.

Secondly, you need to stake all four corners VERY securely in order for it to stay up.  As everyone knows, this doesn't always work that great in some soils (I'm thinking Arches National Park.)  It's part of the design that it's necessary though, especially since the middle doesn't have the proper tension to it to keep it up on it's own.

I am VERY FRUSTRATED that I am going to end up spending about 75.00 in taxes (yes, Viners now have to pay taxes on their items they receive to review, so they are not really free anymore)  for a completely useless dud and lemon.  If only I could return the tent I would in a heartbeat to get my money back.

Even IF it worked right, The tent is nothing fancy and the price tag is a bit high for the size and what you get.  I've gotten cheaper at Sam's Club for a tent twice the size that only takes 20 minutes to install.  NOT worth it.

data.title: Coleman Broadband Mesh Quad Camping Chair
data.description: Bring a comfortable seat with you to the campsite with the Coleman Broadband Mesh Quad Camping Chair. Ideal for camping, picnics, or backyard barbeques, this versatile folding chair offers premium comfort in a roomy yet portable design. It features a breathable mesh back that allows the breeze to cool your back on hot days, plus an integrated mesh cup holder that keeps a refreshing beverage within your reach. Designed to ensure your comfort, the chair features an extra-wide 21.3-inch seat that offers plenty of room to lounge and adjustable arms that let you customize the chair to suit your preferences. The strong steel frame can support up to 250 pounds and withstands seasons of heavy use. And thanks to its collapsible design and convenient carrying bag, this folding chair is easy to store and transport.
data.reviewText:
Nice solid chairs, pack relatively small, relatively light.
No thrills, but also no problems.
They do what they are supposed to do, and do it well.
And cheap.
I'm happy with them - warmly recommended.

data.title: Coleman Camp Stove Carry Case, Medium
data.description: Take your Coleman stove wherever outdoor adventures lead you with the Coleman Camp Stove Carry Case, Medium. Designed to fit Coleman 2-burner stoves measuring up to 21.5 x 14 x 4.8 inches, this rugged carry case made from PVC-coated polyester protects your stove during transportation and storage. Strong web handles provide comfort when toting stoves between campsites, while the durable zipper is designed to keep stoves safe and dry. Two propane pouches with drawstrings securely carry extra fuel canisters (sold separately).
data.reviewText: Fits my Triton with room enough to spare for the griddle and a few extra things in there as well...small cooking utensil sent, and an enamel grill cover (for open fire cooking).  Like how it holds 2 propane tanks.  Quality is decent...zipper has held up so far so good after about 6 trips.

data.title: Coleman 8-Person Tent for Camping | Red Canyon Car Camping Tent
data.description: Measuring 17- by 10-feet, Coleman's Red Canyon tent is spacious enough to comfortably sleep eight campers. Ideal for weekend camping trips with family and friends, the Red Canyon features removable privacy walls, allowing you to create up to three separate rooms.
data.reviewText:
Very large and roomy.  Took two adults and 4 kids, lots of extra room for bags and toys.  The room dividers were ok, nothing more than a thin sheet but my 8 and 9 yr old loved it.

Setup is a two person job, I did put it up once but it is hard to do alone.  No issues with product, great tent and its big!  windows and door were solid as normal, this is the second Colman tent and would never buy anything else.  The floor is tarp like and it always keeps the rain and moisture out.

data.title: Coleman Instant Pop-Up Canopy Tent and Sun Shelter with LED Lighting, 10 x 10 Feet
data.description:
The Coleman Instant Pop-Up Canopy Tent and Sun Shelter with LED Lighting, 10 x 10 Feet, lets you enjoy the outdoors any time of day or night. Great for picnics, barbecues, campouts, and more, this sun shelter is made with UV Guard material that offers UPF 50+ protection from the sun's harmful rays. The lightweight telescoping poles with pinch-free Comfort Grip technology can be extended comfortably without having to reach over your head, allowing you to set up the canopy tent quickly. Once assembled, the canopy offers stable shelter on grass, concrete, and asphalt thanks to its welded steel feet. When the sun goes down, the fun can keep going thanks to the 6 built-in CPX LED lights that supply 400 lumens of illumination. The lighting system can be powered by 4 D-cell batteries or one CPX 6 rechargeable power cartridge (both sold separately). When its time to pack up, the shelter folds down quickly and fits easily inside most cars. Whether you're camping, dining in the backyard, or hosting a wedding reception, the Coleman Instant Canopy makes an ideal companion. The Instant Canopy is Coleman's most innovative canopy, with a three-step setup that takes three minutes or less to pitch.


The canopy is easy to set up via a 3-step, 3-minute process.
No more fiddling with hard-to-interpret pole assemblies or poorly designed attachment systems. More importantly, the canopy comes with four built-in ultra-bright LED fixtures at the top of the shelter. With light emanating out from the ceiling, you can dine at dusk with full overhead illumination and dance after dark without bringing out multiple lanterns. The light system even comes with a convenient dimmer switch, so you can choose low-light ambience all the way up to bright task lighting. The lighting system operates on four D batteries (sold separately), but is also compatible with Coleman's CPX 6 battery power system. The canopy is sturdily constructed, with a 28mm steel frame and a tough cover that's made using UVGuard fabric, which provides a superior 50+ ultraviolet protection factor. And Coleman added pinch-free Comfort Grips to the frame, so you can protect your fingers during setup and takedown. Just a single squeeze locks or releases the frame for fast setup or teardown. The Instant Canopy offers 10 feet by 10 feet of shade and a center height of 9 feet 4 inches. About Coleman
More than 100 years ago, a young man with an entrepreneurial spirit and a better idea began manufacturing lanterns in Wichita, Kansas. His name was W.C. Coleman, and the company he founded would change life in America. A man plagued with such poor vision he sometimes had to ask classmates to read aloud to him, Coleman saw a brilliant light in 1900 in a drugstore window that stopped him in his tracks. He inquired about the light inside and discovered he was able to read even the small print on a medicine bottle by the illumination. The lamps had mantles, not wicks, and were fueled by gasoline under pressure instead of coal oil. Soon afterward, Coleman started a lighting service that offered a "no light, no pay" clause--a big step forward for merchants who were burned by inferior products that rarely worked--and drew substantial interest from businesses that wanted to keep their lights on after dark. In the ensuing years, Coleman expanded its product line well beyond lanterns. The company's current catalog is thick with products that make spending time outdoors a pleasure. There are coolers that keep food and drinks cold for days on end, comfortable airbeds that won't deflate during the night, a complete line of LED lights that last for years, powerful portable grills that cook with an authentic open-grill flame, and much, much more. Coleman has truly fashioned much of our outdoor camping experience, and expects to do so for generations to come.
data.reviewText: Bought for a recent group camping trip, and our whole party immediately wondered how we ever camped without it. Don't know how it compares to the newer version (which has different lights I think) but this one is pretty great. One star off for the power pack being kind of cheesy. It's easy for the battery compartment to open a little, which breaks the electrical connection and might make everyone think the batteries are dead. And the stakes are, of course, complete junk. Get some good ones, at least for the four guylines, and you will be much happier.

data.title: Coleman 42-Can Soft Cooler with Removable Liner & Wheels
data.description: The Coleman 42-Can Wheeled Soft Cooler goes with you to the campsite, the BBQ, sporting events, tailgates and beyond. It features durable wheels and a retractable handle, so it's easy and comfortable to transport to wherever the party is. The cooler is big enough to hold 42 cans, and features plenty of extra storage space in its front zippered pocket, side mesh pockets, and mesh pocket in the lid. There are even bungees on the lid for stowing more extras! The cooler is soft on the outside and has a hard plastic liner inside to protect the contents from being crushed. The liner material is FDA approved for food contact, and is removable for easy cleaning. The Soft Cooler provides to resist odors, mold, and mildew.
data.reviewText: Needed a wheeled portable cooler for our trip to the NJ shore and this one fit the bill. I love the removable liner since I can move things in and out of the house without sand everywhere.  It rolls well the handle is sturdy and it keeps items pretty cool considering ambient air temps were in the low 90s

data.title: Coleman 5-Gallon Solar Shower
data.description: Don't miss a shower  even when you're camping  with the Coleman 5-Gallon Solar Shower. The reservoir holds up to 5 gallons of waterenough for multiple showers. It's constructed of heat-locking material that warms the water when it's left in the sun, even on cool days. The shower features a strong handle for reliable hanging and carrying as well as a handy on/off shower head valve to conserve water.
data.reviewText:
I brought this to MOAB where the dirt blows into every pore of you body. A bath at the end of the day is the perfect thing to crawl into your sleeping bag clean. I found out fine dust can stop the spout from operating as well as it pulled off and detached from the hose. The other thing that seems troubling is to clean it properly and put it away dry inside without mold growing. Coleman should take a lesson from Camelback in having a wide mouth not only to fill it but to clean and dry it properly!
Very disappointed!
data.text:
TITLE:
Coleman 5-Gallon Solar Shower

DESCRIPTION:
Don't miss a shower  even when you're camping  with the Coleman 5-Gallon Solar Shower. The reservoir holds up to 5 gallons of waterenough for multiple showers. It's constructed of heat-locking material that warms the water when it's left in the sun, even on cool days. The shower features a strong handle for reliable hanging and carrying as well as a handy on/off shower head valve to conserve water.

REVIEW:
I brought this to MOAB where the dirt blows into every pore of you body. A bath at the end of the day is the perfect thing to crawl into your sleeping bag clean. I found out fine dust can stop the spout from operating as well as it pulled off and detached from the hose. The other thing that seems troubling is to clean it properly and put it away dry inside without mold growing. Coleman should take a lesson from Camelback in having a wide mouth not only to fill it but to clean and dry it properly!
Very disappointed!

data.title: Coleman Dunnock Cold Weather Adult Sleeping Bag
data.description: Ideal for camping at higher altitudes or in cooler months, the Coleman Dunnock Cold-Weather Sleeping Bag helps you sleep comfortably in temperatures as low as 20F. This sleeping bag features Fiberlock construction that keeps the insulation from shifting, reducing cold spots and increasing durability. A cotton cover and ultra-soft cotton flannel liner also keep you warm, as well as a Thermolock draft tube that prevents heat loss through the zipper. The bottom of the bag unzips to provide extra ventilation on warmer nights. The 2-way patented ZipPlow system plows fabric away from the zipper to prevent snags and frustration. Plus, the Roll Control design and Wrap 'N' Roll integrated packing system make this lightweight backpacking sleeping bag easy to roll and pack. This sleeping bag has a spacious design that can accommodate most people up to 5 feet 11 inches tall and is machine washable for easy cleaning.
data.reviewText:
Other reviewer said it doesn't roll up. It rolls up fine, it has a built in bag type thing sewn on the bottom. I don't know what he meant because even if you can't roll it tight it doesn't matter, you just get it as tight as you can, rap the flap around and tie the 2 strings. Even has built in nylon handle to carry it. I have not used this in cold weather yet but when I do I will update the review. I did lay in it and it is good size and has one small pocket inside for cellphone or wallet. The zipper zips good. Pretty sure this bag is the replacement to the very popular 'coleman colossal' bag. One difference I noticed is this uses 'coletherm insulation' while the colossal used 'Hollofil-808 insulation' Both carry a 5 YEAR warrenty. The colossal looks a little thicker in the pictures but I never owned  one so I cant be sure. I did own one of the coleman bags with the cotton outside for many years that my grandfather gave me. Back then they seemed like they used a 'tougher' cotton on the outside but maybe its because this bag is still new. I want to add that I bought the very popular Teton Sports Celsius XL bag off amazon and did not care for it. The outside is very slippery like a bubble jacket and prown to ripping. Also if its hot and you try to sleep on top of that bag you will sweat bad. Not with this one.

Update--used it all this winter in my 18wheeler and it worked good. Wouldn't use it in weather below like 45* F, the storage flap has proved to be slightly annoying like the other reviewer said but I haven't cut it off yet. The zipper has snagged only a few times. Changing my score from a 5 to 4 star because the outside cotton is not as tough as the older bag I had, its cheaper material. Amazon has lowered the price to 35$ now. for that price it might be a 5star. Not a big selection of oversized sleeping bags.

data.title: Coleman 2-Person Pop-Up Tent
data.description: Great for festivals and car camping, the Coleman 2-Person Pop-Up Tent sets up in as little as 10 seconds, letting you get back to the fun. This camping tent is uniquely designed with preassembled poles, allowing the tent to pop into place in a snap. The taped floor seams keep you dry even when the ground is soaking wet, while the multi-position rainfly allows you to find the perfect balance of protection and ventilation. This instant tent offers room for two camp pads and comes with two integrated storage pockets to help you keep small items organized. This 2-person tent folds flat for simple storage and is lightweight for easy portability.
data.reviewText:
My boyfriend bought this tent at the last minute for a camping trip we took last weekend. We both grew to hate it. It is super easy to set up, it popped open no problem-all we had to do was stake it down. My biggest complaint is the DOOR! The material is pulled so tight that the zipper is nearly impossible to open and close. I am surprised neither of us ripped it out of frustration. Also we got rained on the first night. The tent gets an A+ for no leaks, but every time we opened the door the water would just fall down into our tent---and since the tent was so small on the inside, of course the water went right into our bed. Also a side note about the size of the tent---There is no way you could fit 4 people in there. It barely had enough room for the 2 of us and neither of us are very big.

Folding it down looked confusing, but he seemed to do it pretty easily. I've heard rumors that the springs tend to break after more than 1 use. If we decide to use this tent again I will have to update my review.

data.title: Coleman Crescent Mummy Bag
data.description:
Coleman design and high-tech insulation combine for maximized warmth and superior comfort. The Coleman Crescent Mummy Bag boasts 48 ounces of hollow-core Coletherm insulation, to trap air for warmth and keep you comfortable all the way down to 15 F. Measuring 32"x82", the Crescent bag fits most people up to 6'2". The Diamond ripstop polyester cover is rugged and tear-resistant, with a comfortable polyester liner. Stuff sack storage. The Coleman 8185B558 Crescent 15-degree Tall Sleeping Bag is a comfortable four-season, mummy-style sleeping bag. At 32-inches by 82-inches, the tall-size Crescent fits most body types up to six-feet-two-inches long. With 38 ounces of Hollofil-808 insulation, this extreme-temperature sleeping bag keeps you warm in temperatures down to 15-degrees Fahrenheit. Features include a nylon cover with a nylon liner and a stuff sack. All Coleman sleeping bags are designed with ComfortSmart technology to keep you comfortable. The Crescent's ComfortSmart features include: ZipPlow which plows fabric away from the zipper to prevent snags, Fiberlock which prevents insulation from shifting and increases durability, Thermlock which reduces heat loss through the zipper thereby keeping you warmer, Zipper Glide tailoring which allows smooth zipper operation around the corner, and a Certified Temperature Rating that is independently tested for temperature accuracy. This Coleman sleeping bag is designed to provide the most comfortable overnight stay, at home and away. Manufacturer Warranty
Five-year warranty Amazon.com Sleeping Bag Guide
Sleep Well: Finding the Right Sleeping Bag
Sleeping bag technology has come a long way from the days of cowboy bedrolls. These days, there are a number of high-tech materials and designs available to keep you warm during the coldest outings. Here's a short list of things to keep in mind when you're shopping for a bag: Buy for Cold
It's a safe bet that on at least one of your adventures, the nighttime temperature will drop unexpectedly. That's why it's smart to buy a bag that's rated for the lowest possible temperature you expect to face on your camping and backpacking trips. For summer trips, a bag rated at +35 degrees or higher will likely do the trick. If you like to camp in higher elevations in the summer, or if spring and fall outings are in your future, consider bags rated from +10 to +35. Winter adventurers should look for bags in the -10 to +10 range, while those on serious winter alpine climbs and expeditions will want a bag rated lower than -10. Keep in mind that sleeping bag manufacturers' temperature ratings only estimate the minimum temperature at which the bag will provide warmth. Take these numbers with a grain of salt, as different folks generate different amounts of heat when they sleep. If you're the type who likes to pile on the covers even on warmer nights, go for a bag that's rated ten degrees colder. The opposite is true for "warm" sleepers--a 35-degree bag will probably work for you on a 25-degree night. Goose or No Goose?
The most important component of any sleeping bag is its insulating material. Modern sleeping bags offer two choices: goose down or synthetic. While both materials have advantages and disadvantages, down bags are considered superior because of their phenomenal warmth-to-weight and warmth-to-bulk ratios. While providing great insulation, down is extremely compressible and light. There's a reason why geese can fly and stay warm through the winter! Down also boasts great long-term durability and will typically retain its insulating properties after years of use. All of that said, there are many high-quality synthetic bags on the market and synthetic materials are getting better all the time. While a synthetic bag will weigh somewhat more than a down bag at an equivalent temperature rating, synthetic bags perform better when wet. (Yes, the Achilles heel of down is that it loses all insulating properties when wet.) If your trips take you to wet climates, you may want to consider a synthetic bag for this reason alone. Keep in mind, too, that many people are allergic to down--synthetic bags are non-allergenic. Finally, down is considerably more expensive than synthetic, which might tip the balance for adventurers on a budget. Bags for All Shapes
Sleeping bags come in two basic shapes that reflect their intended use. Mummy-shaped bags offer the best warmth because they conform to the body's contours. This minimizes the amount of body heat the body must put out to maintain a constant temperature. Many mummy bags are offered in women-specific shapes and sizes, as well. Rectangular bags, while they do offer more room to toss and turn, are less thermally efficient because they contain more open air space. Also, they are typically heavier than mummy bags, and are generally not offered with down insulation, making them best suited for car camping or short backpacking trips. Pad Yourself
No matter what kind of bag you choose, a sleeping pad is a required accessory. Not only do they provide much-needed comfort when sleeping on the ground, pads also offer crucial warmth for your backside, as the weight of your body compresses--and renders virtually useless--the sleeping bag insulation that lies beneath you.
data.reviewText: This bag was supposed to be 15 degrees and I took it camping in a low of 45 and I was freezing!  I run cold, and am 5'2''; but I thought that if I bought a 15 degree bag it would have at least kept me warm in a temperature in 45 degrees!  I sent this bag back.  Also, the color wasn't what was in the picture; although, that didn't really bother me.

data.title: Coleman 2000014864 Mosquito Head Net
data.description: Enjoy the outdoors without the pests when you're wearing a Coleman Mosquito Head Net. The fine mesh stops insects from biting, while still allowing you to see nature's beauty. The Mosquito Head Net is designed especially for wide, all-around brim hats, but still fits other styles well. Keep flying insects out when hiking or working in mosquito- or gnat-ridden territory with the Coleman insect head net. The net is designed to fit comfortably over most headgear, and is made with a 1mm maximum netting grade.
data.reviewText: This is a good quality net but it does not fit ideally around the face since it will stick to the face and neck depending on the size of your head and how it is worn.  I recomend wearing it over a hat to allow for a good enough separation between the net and the face.  For the price it is a good net.  It will aliveate insect pestering a great deal but this is not the best net available on the market.

data.title: Coleman Tent for Camping | Elite Montana Tent with Easy Setup
data.description: Stretch out and relax in style with the Coleman Elite Montana 8-Person Lighted Tent. This cutting-edge camping tent features a cabin-like design with a variety of outdoor technologies to enhance any camping trip. The large outdoor tent can fit three queen size air beds, ensuring your whole family has plenty of room. When the sun goes down, you keep the party going with the six built-in CPX LED lights that provide 100 lumens of illumination. The lighting system can be powered by four D batteries (sold separately) or the included CPX 6 rechargeable power cartridge and comes with an energy pack that can power virtually anything with a USB slot. During the day, the auto-roll windows neatly roll down when you unzip them and can open at an angle to let in light but not water when it rains. This 8-person tent is designed with the WeatherTec system that features patented welded floors and inverted seams to help ensure you stay dry. An extended door awning creates a dry space for you to store gear and enter the tent, while the patented hinged door makes it easy to come and go. The heavy-duty tent sets up easily in about 15 minutes thanks to snag-free continuous pole sleeves, a patented pin-and-ring system, and snag-free Insta-Clip suspension. Clip on the included rainfly for added wetness protection, or gaze at the stars through the mesh ceiling.
data.reviewText: Tent will leak if not sealed. Decent tent.
data.text:

Coleman Tent for Camping | Elite Montana Tent with Easy Setup

DESCRIPTION:
Stretch out and relax in style with the Coleman Elite Montana 8-Person Lighted Tent. This cutting-edge camping tent features a cabin-like design with a variety of outdoor technologies to enhance any camping trip. The large outdoor tent can fit three queen size air beds, ensuring your whole family has plenty of room. When the sun goes down, you keep the party going with the six built-in CPX LED lights that provide 100 lumens of illumination. The lighting system can be powered by four D batteries (sold separately) or the included CPX 6 rechargeable power cartridge and comes with an energy pack that can power virtually anything with a USB slot. During the day, the auto-roll windows neatly roll down when you unzip them and can open at an angle to let in light but not water when it rains. This 8-person tent is designed with the WeatherTec system that features patented welded floors and inverted seams to help ensure you stay dry. An extended door awning creates a dry space for you to store gear and enter the tent, while the patented hinged door makes it easy to come and go. The heavy-duty tent sets up easily in about 15 minutes thanks to snag-free continuous pole sleeves, a patented pin-and-ring system, and snag-free Insta-Clip suspension. Clip on the included rainfly for added wetness protection, or gaze at the stars through the mesh ceiling.

REVIEW:
Tent will leak if not sealed. Decent tent.

data.title: Coleman Micro Packer LED Lantern, 100 L
data.description: Grab a lantern and a flashlight in the palm of your hand with the Coleman MicroPacker LED Lantern. The bright, 2-in-1 design snaps from area lighting to directional lighting quite literally in a snap. The bright LED lasts a lifetime, so it never needs to be replaced. The versatile light lasts up to 92 hours, and an extremely lightweight design is only 6 oz.-even with the three AA-cell batteries that power the lantern.
data.reviewText: gives adequate light but the on/off button is hard for my 65 + year old fingers to use.

data.title: Coleman Soft Plush Top Inflated Quickbed
data.description: Take the comfort of an indoor bed with you when you sleep outside on the Coleman Queen Airbed Folding Cot with Side Tables and 4D Battery Pump. Connect the included 4D pump to the Double Lock valve for easy, leak-free inflation. Once you find the perfect firmness, the AirTight system will help keep it that way through the night, since it's factory-tested to be leak-free. The ComfortStrong coil system provides all-night support. It is more than a place to sleep with 2 side tables with cup holders to easily place your drink, a snack, a magazine, or personal items on both sides of the bed. A sewn-in cover keeps the airbed in place on the strong steel frame. When youre ready to pack up, the Double Lock valve lets you deflate the airbed just as easily, and it folds up into a carry bag for easy transport and storage.
data.reviewText: I love coleman stuff.  But my first one came and had a leak somewhere.  I had to get a replacement.  Luckily Amazon is amazing and helped me replace it for free.  The new one is still in the box.  I'm hoping it doesn't leak also.  Otherwise, it's a GREAT air mattress.  Very comfortable and the new valve style is really cool!  You don't have to rush to plug it back up.  It is more of a check valve so when nothing is inserted it plugs itself.
data.text:

DESCRIPTION:
Take the comfort of an indoor bed with you when you sleep outside on the Coleman Queen Airbed Folding Cot with Side Tables and 4D Battery Pump. I love coleman stuff.  But my first one came and had a leak somewhere.  I had to get a replacement.  Luckily Amazon is amazing and helped me replace it for free.  The new one is still in the box. It is more of a check valve so when nothing is inserted it plugs itself.

data.title: Coleman Dome Tent for Camping | Sundome Tent with Easy Setup
data.description: " The Coleman Sundome 4-Person Tent is designed for quick and easy setup, so you can spend more time enjoying the outdoors. Great for camping in warm weather, this backpacking tent is designed with large windows and a ground vent to help push warm air up and out to keep you comfortable. The WeatherTec system with patented welded floors and inverted seams helps ensure you stay dry if the weather turns, while the sturdy frame with Insta-Clip pole attachments stands up to 35+ mph winds. A rainfly with an integrated door awning is included to help keep rain from getting into the tent, and the durable Polyguard 2X double-thick fabric is designed to withstand the elements for reliable use season after season. This dome tent offers enough room for one queen size air mattress and comes with built-in storage pockets to help keep your gear organized. An E-Port makes it easy to bring electrical power inside the tent. The Coleman Sundome 4-Person Tent sets up in only 10 minutes thanks to snag-free continuous pole sleeves and packs away compactly in the included carry bag.
data.reviewText: Decent tent for the price but performed horribly when temps got into the 50's. The two side windows can not be closed and are only covered by the rain fly.  Cold air flowed into the tent easily with no way to retain body heat.  Dew also soaked thru the walls of the tent overnight so I'm unsure of its performance with heavy rain.  Would only recommend for warmer nighttime temperatures.
data.text:
TITLE:
Coleman Dome Tent for Camping | Sundome Tent with Easy Setup

DESCRIPTION:
" The Coleman Sundome 4-Person Tent is designed for quick and easy setup, so you can spend more time enjoying the outdoors. Great for camping in warm weather, this backpacking tent is designed with large windows and a ground vent to help push warm air up and out to keep you comfortable. The WeatherTec system with patented welded floors and inverted seams helps ensure you stay dry if the weather turns, while the sturdy frame with Insta-Clip pole attachments stands up to 35+ mph winds. A rainfly with an integrated door awning is included to help keep rain from getting into the tent, and the durable Polyguard 2X double-thick fabric is designed to withstand the elements for reliable use season after season. This dome tent offers enough room for one queen size air mattress and comes with built-in storage pockets to help keep your gear organized. An E-Port makes it easy to bring electrical power inside the tent. The Coleman Sundome 4-Person Tent sets up in only 10 minutes thanks to snag-free continuous pole sleeves and packs away compactly in the included carry bag.

REVIEW:
Decent tent for the price but performed horribly when temps got into the 50's. The two side windows can not be closed and are only covered by the rain fly.  Cold air flowed into the tent easily with no way to retain body heat.  Dew also soaked thru the walls of the tent overnight so I'm unsure of its performance with heavy rain.  Would only recommend for warmer nighttime temperatures.

data.title: Coleman Camp Axe
data.description: Get the right tool for multiple jobs when you're at the campsite with the Coleman Camp Axe. The sharp edge of the durable drop-forged steel head easily splits wood for a campfire or stove, while the flat edge works wonders for driving tent stakes into hard ground. When it's time to pack up, pulling your tent stakes is easy with the notch cut into the head. Durable and well built, the Coleman Camp Axe is perfect for the campsite, wooded terrain, or the backyard. The axe is equipped with a drop-forged carbon-steel axe head that easily splits wood into kindling, along with a forged steel handle and an ergonomic nonslip grip. With the Coleman Camp Axe, you'll be ready for any camping contingency.
data.reviewText: Hatchet is not sharpened at all! That was disappointing for such a well known brand! Decent weight though.

data.title: Coleman 5-Gallon Solar Shower
data.description: Don't miss a shower  even when you're camping  with the Coleman 5-Gallon Solar Shower. The reservoir holds up to 5 gallons of waterenough for multiple showers. It's constructed of heat-locking material that warms the water when it's left in the sun, even on cool days. The shower features a strong handle for reliable hanging and carrying as well as a handy on/off shower head valve to conserve water.
data.reviewText: I have a serious issue with a couple of design flaws that a flagrant during use. 1st: the handle does not support the weight of the water, it ripped during normal use, we had to Jerry rig it so the handle wouldn't break. Simply keeping the plastic internal handle present the whole way through would eliminate that issue. The 2nd: the hose that this uses gets easily kinked and gravity flow only. The hose is extremely cheap. The only way to prevent this issue is to have the bag high over your  head, but 5gallons of water is not exactly light. Plus, in order to keep water flowing, we had to crouch down low so the hose didn't kink, which in an outdoor setting is kind of gross. I will not purchase again, and will just spend the money to get a higher quality product.

data.title: Coleman 50-Quart Xtreme Wheeled Cooler
data.description: Perfect for any tailgate, barbecue, beach trip or campsite, the Coleman 50-quart Xtreme Cooler has more than enough space for everything you need. With a unique design, this cooler is tall enough to hold 2-liter bottles and large enough for up to 84 cans. Have a seat lidRust proof and leak resistantHolds 2-liter bottles uprightBrand: ColemanQuantity: One (1) coolerColor options: Blue/white or grey/orangeMaterials: PlasticDimensions: 22.6 inches long x 17.6 inches wide x 17.7 inches highModel: 3000001840
data.reviewText: I hate writing negative reviews, I really do, but we are sorely disappointed in this cooler.  We have used it for a week straight and the temps have only gotten over 90 one time and we kept it in the shade all the time. Not once has the ice lasted until the next day but I will say the water stayed cold for 2 days at a time.  The first 2 days we gave it a leeway thinking that once the products were cold inside it would last longer but no, the ice melted in a few hours and left a pile of water.  We have an old junky cheap cooler that works better than this (but it's broken). Maybe we just got a bad one I don't know but I want people to be warned, if you get this make sure you're not depending on it for 5 months straight and then are disappointed once on your trip.  We travel constantly for our business and were hoping this was as good as people said but we were extremely disappointed.

TITLE:
Coleman 50-Quart Xtreme Wheeled Cooler

DESCRIPTION:
Perfect for any tailgate, barbecue, beach trip or campsite, the Coleman 50-quart Xtreme Cooler has more than enough space for everything you need. REVIEW:
I hate writing negative reviews, I really do, but we are sorely disappointed in this cooler.  We have used it for a week straight and the temps have only gotten over 90 one time and we kept it in the shade all the time. Not once has the ice lasted until the next day but I will say the water stayed cold for 2 days at a time.  The first 2 days we gave it a leeway thinking that once the products were cold inside it would last longer but no, the ice melted in a few hours and left a pile of water.  We have an old junky cheap cooler that works better than this (but it's broken). Maybe we just got a bad one I don't know but I want people to be warned, if you get this make sure you're not depending on it for 5 months straight and then are disappointed once on your trip.  We travel constantly for our business and were hoping this was as good as people said but we were extremely disappointed.

data.title: Coleman Bottle Top Propane Stove,Green,6.62" H x 7.81" W x 7.75" L
data.description:
Need a lightweight outdoor stove for backpacking, hunting, or camping? Look no further than the Coleman Portable Bottle-Top Propane Stove, which fits up to an 8-inch pan above a fully adjustable burner for precise temperature control and up to 10,000 BTUs of cooking power. Brew coffee, heat up hot dogs, and so on, knowing you can enjoy up to 2.5 hours of cooking on just one 16.4 oz. propane cylinder (sold separately). PerfectFlow technology keeps that heat steady, even in extreme conditions, and a large base offers stability for easy stirring. When its time to break down the campsite and fit all your gear into a crowded pack, youll be relieved to know that the burner and base separate from the propane cylinder for truly compact packing. Rest assured your ultra-portable stove comes with a 3-year limited warranty. The Coleman 5431A700 One-burner Propane Stove is an easy-to-use portable stove that should meet almost any camp cooking need. The PerfectFlow regulator provides consistent cooking performance by producing a steady fuel stream, even in cold weather, high altitudes, or when fuel is low. Equipped with one 10,000 BTU burner, this fully adjustable stove will last for 2.2 hours on high or up to nine hours on low. The pot supports help shield your cooking flame from the wind, while the large eight-inch burner bowl should fit most any pot. This Coleman one-burner stove can boil a quart of water in just four minutes! This stove operates on one 16.4-ounce cylinder of Coleman propane (not included). Manufacturer Warranty
Limited lifetime warranty
data.reviewText: Good backup for car camping cook system.

data.title: Coleman Hooligan Backpacking Tent
data.description: The Hooligan 2 from Coleman is a 2 person backpacking tent. It features a full coverage coated polyester fly (450mm) that envelops the tent in a cocoon of protection from the harshest weather. A small front vestibule offers some storage space for extra gear and the all mesh body is great for ventilation and stargazing on nights when the weather is clear. The fly seams are sealed at the factory and the rugged 1000D polyethylene floor is a bathtub style (wraps up the sides) for extreme weather protection and has welded seams, even in the corners, to guard against leaks. Set up is with a single pole and thus could not be more simple and quick. Great for beginner backpackers, scouts and those who want to keep it simple.Non Freestanding (1 pole) design is simple to set up and take down.All mesh body for maximum ventilation and comfort and reduces condensation.Zippered back vent in the fly helps to keep the tent comfortable - adjustable through zipper on the back tent wall.Sturdy 11mm (1ea) Shockcorded fiberglass frame for easy set up.All mesh body is perfect for stargazing on nice nights.Full coverage fly is perfect for harsh weather.1 Small storage vestibule - 6 sq. ft. - good for boots etc..1 interior storage pocket.Easy convenient continuous pole sleeve construction.Pin and ring pole to body connection is fast and simple.External guy points to keep the tent stable in storms and high winds.Instructions sewn to carry bag so they are easy to find and hard to lose.All carry sacks and stakes included.Includes convenient flashlight loop on the ceiling.Fire retardant.Import..
data.reviewText:
The Coleman Hooligan 3 is an excellent tent for less-than-ideal spring/summer/fall conditions, but design elements keep me from recommending this tent for all-around use. The positive features actually can be negatives under certain circumstances. I rate this tent at 3.5 stars for general purpose use, and 3.75 for those who want a tent better able to handle tough weather. If the zippers and the multitude of lightweight Coleman-issue stakes were all sturdier, I'd make that a 4.

The Hooligan is similar to a two pole Coleman Sundome tent constructed primarily of netting covered by a complete, waterproof fly with a good sized vestibule. There are none of the "windows" common to the Sundome family of tents; airflow through the tent is via the door/dome/side Ventilation openings. The design makes it impossible to close off the side vents, rendering this tent impractical for very cold weather use. With 13 stakes necessary for the fly, and 4 for the tent itself, setting it up in rocky or very sandy soils could be a bit of a challenge. Otherwise, actual set-up is straightforward (a usage tip-do NOT stake the tent out prior to setting up the main poles; before you do, check the tension across the front of the tent, as I found that a lot of strain can be placed on the tent zipper if you don't proceed in this manner, and the zipper itself is a little on the wimpy side). With the vestibule, most folks will need to do a double-dip or drop to hands and knees to enter the Hooligan through the vestibule and door combination. With a 58" height, standing up inside is out of the question for most of us, but that's to be expected in a small tent. At 8' x 7', the tent can handle a double size air bed, with some room to spare, making it adequate for 2 people (ok, 2 humans and one stinky dog), but forget about the three person designation unless you leave all of your gear outside.

The 13 stake fly covers the inner tent nearly to the ground and stays down tight,even in a strong wind. The dual shell design keeps water from wicking onto items touching the interior sides of the tent, and keeps condensation to a minimum. The now ubiquitous "bathtub" bottom really works; the waterproof poly floor stayed dry even when we recently pitched the tent in a mud puddle. You can get plenty of air going through the tent when it warms up by controlling the upper vent and front door openings. The lack of "windows" doesn't seem to matter much with this tent; the glow provided by the bright over-fly provides light even on a gloomy day (and on a recent trip to the Trinity Alps, allowed enough starlight through to be able to see in the middle of the night). The vestibule is big enough to be truly practical; a couple of day packs and pairs of shoes can be stored there leaving plenty of access room for the main tent (note, however, that the vestibule does not have a built-in floor; a piece of tarp will help keep your items dry).

Updated 6/2011:

We're now in our second season with the Hooligan, and it's proven to be a good choice for us. We've used it in Lassen
N.P., the Trinity Alps and throughout the California Redwoods. It's not the easiest tent to set up, but it handles
weather, is small enough to place in a relatively tiny area, yet the vestibule is big enough to be truly useful as a mud
room, dressing room, and storage area. Experience indicates that the aquisition of strong, steel stakes is essential to
insure successful performance of the tent. This is a relatively minimal additional investment to make in order to obtain
an inexpensive but tough shelter capable of standing up to variable weather conditions. For the reasons I stated above, I
stand by my original rating for the tent; it's just not going to be satisfactory for everyone. But for those who need or
want that next step up and are willing to invest some extra effort in set-up and tear down, a solid value at the price.

data.title: Coleman Camp Stove Carry Case, Small
data.description: Take your Coleman stove wherever outdoor adventures lead you with the Coleman Camp Stove Carry Case, Small. Designed to fit small Coleman 2-burner stoves measuring up to 20.4 x 12 x 4.5 inches, this rugged carry case made from PVC-coated polyester protects your stove during transportation and storage. Strong web handles provide comfort when toting stoves between campsites, while the durable zipper is designed to keep stoves safe and dry. Two propane pouches with drawstrings securely carry extra fuel canisters (sold separately).
data.reviewText: It will do the job but i wish it was padded a little better. I expected a little more from Coleman.

Great for beginner backpackers, scouts and those who want to keep it simple.Non Freestanding (1 pole) design is simple to set up and take down.All mesh body for maximum ventilation and comfort and reduces condensation.Zippered back vent in the fly helps to keep the tent comfortable - adjustable through zipper on the back tent wall.Sturdy 11mm (1ea) Shockcorded fiberglass frame for easy set up.All mesh body is perfect for stargazing on nice nights.Full coverage fly is perfect for harsh weather.1 Small storage vestibule - 6 sq. ft. - good for boots etc..1 interior storage pocket.Easy convenient continuous pole sleeve construction.Pin and ring pole to body connection is fast and simple.External guy points to keep the tent stable in storms and high winds.Instructions sewn to carry bag so they are easy to find and hard to lose.All carry sacks and stakes included.Includes convenient flashlight loop on the ceiling.Fire retardant.Import..
data.reviewText:
I paid a little over $50 for this tent and I really like it. I used it for a weekend so far and it kept me warm, dry, and had plenty of room. I'm 6'3" and have enough room to stand halfway up which makes it easy to change inside. I have never set up a tent before and set this up with no instructions in less than 10 minutes and set it up in less than 5 the 2nd time. The single pole design makes it so easy. There is no footprint with it, i don't think it needs one but better safe than sorry, so i cut up some gardening plastic and am using that. The tent stakes are crappy and small. They are difficult to push into hard ground and bend wayyy too easy. The rainfly kept water out nicely. most of it beaded up but i noticed that there were some dark spots on the material after it sat for about an hour. It doesn't pack into the bag it comes with very easily and takes quite a bit of work. probably not meant for backpacking but comparing it to something that costs $300 that doesn't have as much room and takes longer to put up it is a really great tent. BUY ONE!!

For those that want a quick review:

Pros:
  - Easy and quick set up.
  - Plenty of space inside.
  - Nice FULL rainfly. goes almost all the way to the ground all the way around the tent.
  - Inexpensive

Cons:
  - Nothing to hang a fan or light from on the inside.
  - Rain fly could be a little more waterproof.
  - Cheap tent stakes.
  - Fairly heavy at about 6 lbs
  - Difficult to pack away
data.text:
TITLE:
Coleman Hooligan Backpacking Tent

DESCRIPTION:
The Hooligan 2 from Coleman is a 2 person backpacking tent. It features a full coverage coated polyester fly (450mm) that envelops the tent in a cocoon of protection from the harshest weather. A small front vestibule offers some storage space for extra gear and the all mesh body is great for ventilation and stargazing on nights when the weather is clear. The fly seams are sealed at the factory and the rugged 1000D polyethylene floor is a bathtub style (wraps up the sides) for extreme weather protection and has welded seams, even in the corners, to guard against leaks. Set up is with a single pole and thus could not be more simple and quick. Great for beginner backpackers, scouts and those who want to keep it simple.Non Freestanding (1 pole) design is simple to set up and take down.All mesh body for maximum ventilation and comfort and reduces condensation.Zippered back vent in the fly helps to keep the tent comfortable - adjustable through zipper on the back tent wall.Sturdy 11mm (1ea) Shockcorded fiberglass frame for easy set up.All mesh body is perfect for stargazing on nice nights.Full coverage fly is perfect for harsh weather.1 Small storage vestibule - 6 sq. ft. - good for boots etc..1 interior storage pocket.Easy convenient continuous pole sleeve construction.Pin and ring pole to body connection is fast and simple.External guy points to keep the tent stable in storms and high winds.Instructions sewn to carry bag so they are easy to find and hard to lose.All carry sacks and stakes included.Includes convenient flashlight loop on the ceiling.Fire retardant.Import..

REVIEW:
I paid a little over $50 for this tent and I really like it. I used it for a weekend so far and it kept me warm, dry, and had plenty of room. I'm 6'3" and have enough room to stand halfway up which makes it easy to change inside. I have never set up a tent before and set this up with no instructions in less than 10 minutes and set it up in less than 5 the 2nd time. The single pole design makes it so easy. There is no footprint with it, i don't think it needs one but better safe than sorry, so i cut up some gardening plastic and am using that. The tent stakes are crappy and small. They are difficult to push into hard ground and bend wayyy too easy. The rainfly kept water out nicely. most of it beaded up but i noticed that there were some dark spots on the material after it sat for about an hour. It doesn't pack into the bag it comes with very easily and takes quite a bit of work. probably not meant for backpacking but comparing it to something that costs $300 that doesn't have as much room and takes longer to put up it is a really great tent. BUY ONE!!

For those that want a quick review:

Pros:
  - Easy and quick set up.
  - Plenty of space inside.
  - Nice FULL rainfly. goes almost all the way to the ground all the way around the tent.
  - Inexpensive

Cons:
  - Nothing to hang a fan or light from on the inside.
  - Rain fly could be a little more waterproof.
  - Cheap tent stakes.
  - Fairly heavy at about 6 lbs
  - Difficult to pack away

data.title: Coleman 8-Person Camping Tent with Built-in Closet | Tenaya Lake Cabin Tent with Fast Pitch Setup
data.description: Enjoy some comforts from home while camping with the Coleman Tenaya Lake Fast Pitch 8-Person Cabin Tent with Closet. This camping tent includes a built-in closet with shelves and a hanger bar, making it easy to keep your clothes and gear organized without having to dig through your backpack. Thanks to pre-attached, color-coded top poles and hub, fast-fit feet, and snag-free Insta-Clip suspension, this cabin tent with the Fast Pitch system assembles 55 percent quicker than similar Coleman tents with a conventional setup. The WeatherTec system with patented welded floors and inverted seams helps ensure you and your gear stay dry, while the included rainfly offers extra weather protection. This backpacking tent offers enough room for two queen air beds and comes with a room divider for added privacy and storage pockets to keep your items stored away. An E-Port lets you easily bring power inside the tent. A hinged door lets you enter and exit the tent effortlessly, and Illumiline reflective guy lines offer greater visibility at night, so you wont accidentally trip in the dark. And when its time to pack up, the 8-person tent fits neatly inside the included wheeled carry bag for easy storage and transportation.
data.reviewText: This tent is extremely large. The tent is not as easy as you would think it would be or is it make it sound. It's a bit of a pain. The bag it comes in was very nice easy to transport. I had a steam that was coming apart when I first set it up at the campsite. I could have just been that model. It definitely takes time to set up. I actually returned it because of the seam coming apart. Like I said before I think the quality is really good it just could have been this particular one that I. I bought it new. but if you have a large family or you're looking for a large tent it is very nice just understand that it takes time to set up.

data.title: Coleman Jug With Water Carrier, 5 Gallons, Blue
data.description: Coleman, 5 gallon, blue, poly Lite water carrier, molded in handle & hand grip, fast flow spigot stores on the handle when not in use, non-insulated, polyethylene case resists scratches & dents. Comfortably port water to your campsite, picnic, boat, and beyond with the Coleman 5-gallon water carrier. The carrier features a tough polyethylene construction that's scratch- and impact-resistant, helping it hold up to rough handling. More importantly, the carrier's fast-flow spigot delivers instant water access; no more struggling to tip the carrier each time you fill up your water bottle. The carrier also includes a built-in molded handle and handgrip for comfortable, easy carrying.
data.reviewText:
I purchased a Coleman Water Carrier 15+ years ago and it lasted until the plastic container cracked from age.
The spigot outlasted the container.  Being pleased with the product, I decided to replace it with the same model. My mistake.
The brand new spigot broke on the very first use. Absolutely no way to repair the break other than replace the spigot.
Add my 2 cents to the chorus.

I like the design and features but, Coleman PLEASE fix the issue with breaking spigots.

data.title: Coleman 9-Can Soft Cooler with Removable Liner
data.description: Keep food and drinks cold with the Coleman Soft Cooler. This compact, portable cooler bag holds up to nine cans and offers plenty of space for a family picnic or game-day snacks. Heat-welded seams in the zippered main compartment prevent leaks and maintain a cold environment, while the removable hard plastic liner fits snugly inside the soft cooler and allows you to easily dump out any ice melt. When not using the hard plastic liner, you can use ice substitutes to keep contents cold and dry. The cooler bag's easy-clean interior has an antimicrobial lining to resist formation of mold, mildew, fungus, and odor. A zippered front pocket, pocket under the lid, and two side mesh pockets make it easy to carry water bottles, snacks, silverware, and other accessories. You can also secure items to the top with the convenient bungee cords built into the lid. An adjustable shoulder strap lets you comfortably carry the cooler anywhere you go.
data.reviewText: I bought this for a day trip to the beach on Caya Costa.  I checked it out and was able to fit several bottles of water (or soda) a couple of sandwiches, several ice boxes, and some snack bars in the front pocket with my phone.  Should be plenty for six hour beach trip.  Very light as well, and easy to carry.
data.text:
TITLE:
Coleman 9-Can Soft Cooler with Removable Liner

DESCRIPTION:
Keep food and drinks cold with the Coleman Soft Cooler. This compact, portable cooler bag holds up to nine cans and offers plenty of space for a family picnic or game-day snacks. Heat-welded seams in the zippered main compartment prevent leaks and maintain a cold environment, while the removable hard plastic liner fits snugly inside the soft cooler and allows you to easily dump out any ice melt. When not using the hard plastic liner, you can use ice substitutes to keep contents cold and dry. The cooler bag's easy-clean interior has an antimicrobial lining to resist formation of mold, mildew, fungus, and odor. REVIEW:
I bought this for a day trip to the beach on Caya Costa.  I checked it out and was able to fit several bottles of water (or soda) a couple of sandwiches, several ice boxes, and some snack bars in the front pocket with my phone.  Should be plenty for six hour beach trip.  Very light as well, and easy to carry.

data.title: Coleman Propane Grill | RoadTrip LXE Portable Gas Grill
data.description: Stop manhandling those heavy, bulky backyard beasts and show off as the grill master with the Coleman RoadTrip LXE Propane Grill. It's easy to tote around and set up. Just fold it up, roll it with one hand to your favorite cooking spot and set it up in a matter of seconds with the lift and lock system. The grill comes equipped with durable cast-iron grilling surfaces. (Coleman RoadTrip pot support and griddles are available separately.) When you're ready to grill, push a button to ignite the InstaStart matchless lighting system. Cook for more than an hour on one propane cylinder with 20,000 BTUs of power on 285 sq. in. (1838.7 sq. cm) of cooking surface. It's perfect for BBQs, picnics, tailgating, camping or a night on the patio. When you're ready to leave, it folds down small enough to fit in the back of most car trunks.
data.reviewText: Excellent grill! Works great, stores and cleans easily! One small propane canister has already lasted for ten meals and still going!

data.title: Coleman Camp Axe
data.description: Get the right tool for multiple jobs when you're at the campsite with the Coleman Camp Axe. The sharp edge of the durable drop-forged steel head easily splits wood for a campfire or stove, while the flat edge works wonders for driving tent stakes into hard ground. When it's time to pack up, pulling your tent stakes is easy with the notch cut into the head. Durable and well built, the Coleman Camp Axe is perfect for the campsite, wooded terrain, or the backyard. The axe is equipped with a drop-forged carbon-steel axe head that easily splits wood into kindling, along with a forged steel handle and an ergonomic nonslip grip. With the Coleman Camp Axe, you'll be ready for any camping contingency.
data.reviewText: It's a used camping axe.  Look at my pictures.  I didn't see anything about a used or refurbished axe.  Do not buy.

data.title: Coleman Camp Axe
data.description: Get the right tool for multiple jobs when you're at the campsite with the Coleman Camp Axe. The sharp edge of the durable drop-forged steel head easily splits wood for a campfire or stove, while the flat edge works wonders for driving tent stakes into hard ground. When it's time to pack up, pulling your tent stakes is easy with the notch cut into the head. Durable and well built, the Coleman Camp Axe is perfect for the campsite, wooded terrain, or the backyard. The axe is equipped with a drop-forged carbon-steel axe head that easily splits wood into kindling, along with a forged steel handle and an ergonomic nonslip grip. With the Coleman Camp Axe, you'll be ready for any camping contingency.
data.reviewText:
my hatchet was purchased for a general backyard tool. but when I got it home, I noticed this small "thumb nail" size white warning label. "This item contains a substance that has been found the be harmful to humans. use gloves."

Upon further investigation, it seems that the hatchet contains LEAD. Apparently California has legislature that prevent any company from selling items with lead in its composition without "labelling" the item as such.

I promptly returned this product.

Upon further investigation, it seems that the hatchet contains LEAD. Apparently California has legislature that prevent any company from selling items with lead in its composition without "labelling" the item as such.

I promptly returned this product.

data.title: Coleman Trailhead II Cot
data.reviewText: When I got them and opened them up 1 was already damaged. This isn't a review about the seller as they are willing to help by exchanging however I need them and I need them now. Don't have time for all of that. I have to wire the broken one together and hope it holds. So far it has. The greater concern I have is the taughtness of the fabric. Pretty taught when new. A 200lb guy will have this stretched out in about 2 nights, my back is starting to hit the aluminum frame below. I will not ever again purchase any item made by Coleman PERIOD.  Been burnt on everything I've bought of there's for years and I'm done with them. I would recommend spending a little extra. It'll go a long way in the end. These are not military grade. Come to think of it pretty sure the US Military doesn't use anything made by Coleman. I could be wrong tho.

data.title: Coleman Exponent Multi-Fuel Stove
data.description: The Coleman Multi-Fuel Stove offers sturdy, compact design and versatile, powerful performance. The integrated fuel tank operates on your choice of Coleman fuel, unleaded gasoline or kerosene (kerosene generator included). The sturdy fold-out legs keep the stove stable, while burner bowl and pot supports help protect the flame even in windy conditions. Precise flame control allows you to go from simmer to boil. Stove weighs 21.6 ounces. Get ready to cook with the Coleman Exponent 550B725 Multi-Fuel camp stove, which weighs 21.6 ounces. Fill up the integrated tank one time and use it all weekend. It operates on either Coleman fuel or unleaded gasoline, and can also operate on kerosene with an optional generator. Thanks to Coleman's PerfectFlow feature, it's regulated for a constant, consistent burn (without flaring or flickering). Other features include fold-out legs and wind-protecting burner bowel and pot supports. The Coleman Exponent 550-6771 Maintenance Kit is a recommended accessory.
data.reviewText: Got this stove for it's simmer capability. A bit pricey and has plastic parts. Seems to work alright with Coleman fuel.  I did not try the multifuel capability. It is still too big to nest in cook kit.  I prefer the older Canadian Coleman that is no longer made.

REVIEW: Got this stove for it's simmer capability. A bit pricey and has plastic parts. Seems to work alright with Coleman fuel. I did not try the multifuel capability. It is still too big to nest in cook kit. I prefer the older Canadian Coleman that is no longer made.

data.title: Coleman Hooligan Backpacking Tent
data.description: The Hooligan 2 from Coleman is a 2 person backpacking tent. It features a full coverage coated polyester fly (450mm) that envelops the tent in a cocoon of protection from the harshest weather. A small front vestibule offers some storage space for extra gear and the all mesh body is great for ventilation and stargazing on nights when the weather is clear. The fly seams are sealed at the factory and the rugged 1000D polyethylene floor is a bathtub style (wraps up the sides) for extreme weather protection and has welded seams, even in the corners, to guard against leaks. Set up is with a single pole and thus could not be more simple and quick. Great for beginner backpackers, scouts and those who want to keep it simple.Non Freestanding (1 pole) design is simple to set up and take down.All mesh body for maximum ventilation and comfort and reduces condensation.Zippered back vent in the fly helps to keep the tent comfortable - adjustable through zipper on the back tent wall.Sturdy 11mm (1ea) Shockcorded fiberglass frame for easy set up.All mesh body is perfect for stargazing on nice nights.Full coverage fly is perfect for harsh weather.1 Small storage vestibule - 6 sq. ft. - good for boots etc..1 interior storage pocket.Easy convenient continuous pole sleeve construction.Pin and ring pole to body connection is fast and simple.External guy points to keep the tent stable in storms and high winds.Instructions sewn to carry bag so they are easy to find and hard to lose.All carry sacks and stakes included.Includes convenient flashlight loop on the ceiling.Fire retardant.Import..
data.reviewText:
I like the tent a lot, but there are a few features I wish it had:
1. Clips instead of the sleeves for the poles in the top of the tent (lighter, less likely to get gross over time, easier).
2. Something to hang a light from at the top of the tent.  There is nothing (NOTHING!) anywhere.  Seems an easy addition that wouldn't break Coleman's bank.
3. Some medium between full open windows where any neighbors will see all and the stifling fly completely covering.  There is seriously no in-between, which is silly.
Overall fine for tent camping, but laughable when described as a backpacking tent; waaaay too heavy for that!  Suited my purpose for tent camping, except really do wish I had a bit more options with the fly either being suffocating or exposing my every action to those around me.
In the end, it serves its purpose, but not sure the good outweighs the bad and I probably would look for something else if it had it to do over.

Coleman Hooligan Backpacking Tent

The Hooligan 2 from Coleman is a 2 person backpacking tent. It features a full coverage coated polyester fly (450mm) that envelops the tent in a cocoon of protection from the harshest weather. A small front vestibule offers some storage space for extra gear and the all mesh body is great for ventilation and stargazing on nights when the weather is clear. The fly seams are sealed at the factory and the rugged 1000D polyethylene floor is a bathtub style (wraps up the sides) for extreme weather protection and has welded seams, even in the corners, to guard against leaks. Set up is with a single pole and thus could not be more simple and quick. Great for beginner backpackers, scouts and those who want to keep it simple.Non Freestanding (1 pole) design is simple to set up and take down.All mesh body for maximum ventilation and comfort and reduces condensation.Zippered back vent in the fly helps to keep the tent comfortable - adjustable through zipper on the back tent wall.Sturdy 11mm (1ea) Shockcorded fiberglass frame for easy set up.All mesh body is perfect for stargazing on nice nights.Full coverage fly is perfect for harsh weather.1 Small storage vestibule - 6 sq. ft. - good for boots etc..1 interior storage pocket.Easy convenient continuous pole sleeve construction.Pin and ring pole to body connection is fast and simple.External guy points to keep the tent stable in storms and high winds.Instructions sewn to carry bag so they are easy to find and hard to lose.All carry sacks and stakes included.Includes convenient flashlight loop on the ceiling.Fire retardant.Import..

REVIEW:
I like the tent a lot, but there are a few features I wish it had:
1. Clips instead of the sleeves for the poles in the top of the tent (lighter, less likely to get gross over time, easier).
2. Something to hang a light from at the top of the tent.  There is nothing (NOTHING!) anywhere.  Seems an easy addition that wouldn't break Coleman's bank.
