Two inches of insulation in the lid and case provide superior cold retention: it will keep food, beverages and ice for up to 6 days at outside temperatures up to 90 F. This 50-quart cooler can hold up to 72 cans with ice. The cooler's large, sturdy wheels are designed for pulling the cooler through rough terrain. A comfortable tow handle provides one-handed portability, while two-way carry handles make it easy to lift or move. The convenient hinged lid sports four built-in drink holders, each a reliable 2" deep. The lid is designed to accommodate 24" elastic cords (sold separately) for additional storage. A rustproof, leak-resistant Channel Drain provides easy, no-tilt draining of melted ice or other liquids. Color: ice green. Take your Coleman 50-Quart Wheeled Ultimate Cooler on the road, to the job site, on vacation, or to game day. With plenty of food and beverage storage space, four stay-put beverage holders, all-terrain wheels, and an easy-grasp tow handle, this ice green cooler can handle any event. The chest cooler stores ice for up to six days at temperatures of up to 90 degrees and can hold 72 beverage cans with ice. Made of durable plastic, the exterior resists scratching and denting, and the interior features a Channel Drain for easy, no-tilt draining and clean-up. This models lid can be fitted with 24-inch cords (not included) for additional dry storage--lawn chairs, picnic blankets, beach towels--on top. About Coleman
The Coleman Company has been creating and innovating products for recreational outdoor use since W.C. Coleman started selling gasoline-powered lanterns in 1900. Inventor of the hugely popular fold-up camp stove, Coleman developed a plastic liner for his galvanized steel coolers in 1957--the birth of the modern cooler--and the company has been improving their utility and design ever since. The array of products that bear the Coleman name now includes just about everything you might need to work or play outdoors, from tents and sleeping bags to boats, backpacks, and furniture. REVIEW: doesn't have that great of a seal. can turn it upside down and the lid will flip open without any hesitation. highly disappointed in the product. I placed a flashlight inside the box and was able to see a lot of light coming thru. not sure how this will keep anything cold more than a day or two at the most. I'm blessed that there is an easy return policy from the seller. I wouldn't recommend this series of Coleman coolers.

data.title: Coleman Quad LED Lantern Special Edition Ultra Bright 190 Lumens, Red
data.description:
Take the convenience of four lights in one with you with the Coleman Quad LED Lantern. Together the lantern shines 190 ultra-bright lumens for up to 75 hours. During an emergency, pop one of the four lights off the base for a personal flashlight. They recharge once theyre back on the base for hands-free light. The 24 LEDs last a lifetime, so they never need replacing and the lantern runs cool so its always safe to touch, even when its been on for hours. Combining an area light with four portable lanterns in a single clever unit, the Coleman LED Quad lantern is a versatile choice for indoor or outdoor use. The Quad is equipped with four removable LED panels that hold 24 LED lights total. Users can remove any of the four panels and use them independently, such as when needing a portable light for the bathroom or tent, or they can keep all four attached to the lantern and produce 190 total lumens of light. As a result, the Quad is the perfect light for group camping trips and any other occasion where folks might splinter off into smaller groups during the night. Each of the panels is a fully functioning light, with six 5mm white LEDs, a rechargeable NiMH battery, an independent on/off switch, and a top handle for carrying or hanging. The NiMH battery--which recharges when stored in the lantern base--provides up to 1.5 hours of light on its own. The lantern base, meanwhile, requires eight D-cell batteries (not included), which provide an approximate run time of 75 hours for the entire lantern. Other features include a main on/off switch located on top of the lantern, a light range of 26 feet, and a limited five-year warranty. About Coleman
More than 100 years ago, a young man with an entrepreneurial spirit and a better idea began manufacturing lanterns in Wichita, Kansas. His name was W.C. Coleman, and the company he founded would change life in America. A man plagued with such poor vision he sometimes had to ask classmates to read aloud to him, Coleman saw a brilliant light in 1900 in a drugstore window that stopped him in his tracks. He inquired about the light inside and discovered he was able to read even the small print on a medicine bottle by the illumination. The lamps had mantles, not wicks, and were fueled by gasoline under pressure instead of coal oil. Soon afterward, Coleman started a lighting service that offered a "no light, no pay" clause--a big step forward for merchants who were burned by inferior products that rarely worked--and drew substantial interest from businesses that wanted to keep their lights on after dark. In the ensuing years, Coleman expanded its product line well beyond lanterns. The company's current catalog is thick with products that make spending time outdoors a pleasure. There are coolers that keep food and drinks cold for days on end, comfortable airbeds that won't deflate during the night, a complete line of LED lights that last for years, powerful portable grills that cook with an authentic open-grill flame, and much, much more. Coleman has truly fashioned much of our outdoor camping experience, and expects to do so for generations to come.
data.reviewText:
The light it puts out is that ugly ultra white LED light that use to be standard for LEDS.  It's very annoying to use and I actually really dislike the pattern they put out.  You blind anyone not directly behind the light.  Its not a light you'd be OK with standing in front of by any means.  The required 8 D sized batteries is VERY annoying.  It skyrockets the operation cost.  For the cost, it should have a built in rechargeable battery.

I bought this based on so many glowing (no pun) reviews, however I am not a fan of the light it puts out or having to track down all four lights the kids always forget to bring back.  Individual LED flashlights would be far more effective.  I simply wouldn't ever use this for its lantern features.  The light color and blinding LED is far to annoying.

data.title: Coleman 9-Can Soft Cooler with Removable Liner
data.description: Keep food and drinks cold with the Coleman Soft Cooler. This compact, portable cooler bag holds up to nine cans and offers plenty of space for a family picnic or game-day snacks. Heat-welded seams in the zippered main compartment prevent leaks and maintain a cold environment, while the removable hard plastic liner fits snugly inside the soft cooler and allows you to easily dump out any ice melt. When not using the hard plastic liner, you can use ice substitutes to keep contents cold and dry. The cooler bag's easy-clean interior has an antimicrobial lining to resist formation of mold, mildew, fungus, and odor. A zippered front pocket, pocket under the lid, and two side mesh pockets make it easy to carry water bottles, snacks, silverware, and other accessories. You can also secure items to the top with the convenient bungee cords built into the lid. An adjustable shoulder strap lets you comfortably carry the cooler anywhere you go.
data.reviewText: Haven't used it yet as it's a gift but it seems pretty nice. Can't really go wrong with Coleman

data.title: Coleman Palmetto Cool Weather Adult Sleeping Bag
data.description: The Coleman Palmetto Sleeping Bag is a rectangular, cool weather sleeping bag made for camping temperatures between 30 and 50 degrees. Perfect for Family camping!.ThermoLock construction reduces heat loss through the zipper, keeping you warmer.ZipperGlide tailoring allows smooth zipper operation around the corners.QuickCord for easy, no tie closure.ComfortCuff surrounds your face with softness.Commercial Machine washable.
data.reviewText:
Okay, I bought these for a weekend camping trip that was supposed to be 40 degrees fahrenheit at the lowest. During the night there was a sudden unexpected freeze and it dropped down to 25! However, we didn't even notice because we were snug in these sleeping bags. When I woke up, there was a layer of ice on the sleeping bag, but I was still warm!
These are awesome, especially for the price! The color was more of a cranberry/burgundy and they're a little tricky to roll up, but I would definitely recommend this sleeping bag.

data.title: Coleman Propane Grill | RoadTrip LXE Portable Gas Grill
data.description: Stop manhandling those heavy, bulky backyard beasts and show off as the grill master with the Coleman RoadTrip LXE Propane Grill. It's easy to tote around and set up. Just fold it up, roll it with one hand to your favorite cooking spot and set it up in a matter of seconds with the lift and lock system. The grill comes equipped with durable cast-iron grilling surfaces. (Coleman RoadTrip pot support and griddles are available separately.) When you're ready to grill, push a button to ignite the InstaStart matchless lighting system. Cook for more than an hour on one propane cylinder with 20,000 BTUs of power on 285 sq. in. (1838.7 sq. cm) of cooking surface. It's perfect for BBQs, picnics, tailgating, camping or a night on the patio. When you're ready to leave, it folds down small enough to fit in the back of most car trunks.
data.reviewText: I love my Coleman Road Trip Grill! along with a great price! it is always left in the Toy Hauler,and ready for the next cook off.

data.title: Coleman 8-Person Tent | Instant Family Tent
data.reviewText:
BUYER BEWARE: We own three Coleman tents and several of their other products generally, their quality is excellent. This particular tent was manufactured incorrectly.

One of the poles was assembled upside down so it won't slide into the groove to either snap into place or collapse. We've used this tent twice and have been able to get it to stand up with the defective pole by using guy lines to compensate. We thought we were doing something wrong but couldn't figure out what.

The real problem comes in taking it down. You can't slide the pole back in. We ended up loosening the screw and hammering it in requiring 4 people to hold it, at 5am, not fun. Instead of being the fastest tent to set up and take down it takes exponentially longer.

You won't find out about this defect until you set it up and try to take it down again. We might be able to take it apart and turn the pole to line up correctly and then reattach it. The problem is that its on the top part of the tent which will require assembling it and standing on two ladders while leaning over the tent. I can't figure out a way to do this safely and frankly, I don't think I should have to.

Coleman and/or Amazon, do you stand by your products?

UPDATE: Coleman is working with us to replace the tent. I'll update here if/when things are resolved.

The problem is that its on the top part of the tent which will require assembling it and standing on two ladders while leaning over the tent. data.title: Coleman One-Mantle Kerosene Lantern
data.description:
Illuminate your outdoor adventures with the Coleman One-Mantle Kerosene Lantern. This durable camping lantern features a one-mantle design that casts up to 784 lumens of high-intensity light. You can light the lantern with a quick match strike and dial in the desired brightness level with the adjustable dimmer knob. The high-temperature globe resists cracking and is protected by built-in spring clips, and the porcelain-coated ventilator helps resist corrosion and rust. A bail handle makes it easy to carry or hang the lantern, and the fuel tank provides a steady base when the lantern is placed on a table or other surface. This outdoor lantern lasts up to 5.75 hours on high with 2 pints of kerosene (sold separately). It comes with a pre-installed #11 mantle and a plastic bottle to help fill the pre-heater. Sometimes, you just can't beat a classic kerosene lantern to light up your campsite. This model is iconic lantern maker Coleman's brightest such device, offering high-intensity light output with the included #11 mantles. It provides up to 5.5 hours of burn time on one two-pint kerosene tank, and -- thanks to an easy-to-operate, single-knob control -- it's as intuitive to use as it is durable. The fuel tank is coated on the interior to hold up to repeated use, and a "porcelain-ized" ventilator resists rust. Includes an automatic tip cleaner. The Coleman Limited Warranty
All Coleman products have a warranty against defects in material and workmanship, and details are included with your purchase. About Coleman Outdoor Products
More than 100 years ago, an entrepreneurial young man named W.C. Coleman had an idea for manufacturing better lanterns in Wichita, Kansas. A century later, Coleman's current catalog features a wide-ranging array of products that make spending time outside a pleasure. The company crafts coolers that keep food and drinks cold for days, airbeds that are comfortable and won't deflate during the night, a complete line of bright and long-lasting LED lights, powerful portable grills that cook with an authentic open-grill flame, and more.
data.reviewText: I love Coleman lanterns. This seems to be built with the quality of old. The mantle did char up after an hour of burning. I am using the high quality clear fuel that Lowes sells for heaters. I bought better quality mantles on eBay. Then I bought a new old stock glass chimney to replace the made in China one with the large made in China lettering. The replacement says made in USA, just like the rest of the lantern. I am now using a bit of Coleman gas mixed in, which some call the Amish mix. The color of the light is a bit yellow when compared to the white color of my Coleman gas lantern.

data.title: Coleman 12 oz. Enamel Mug
data.description: Grab a refreshing drink at the campsite, BBQ or picnic with an impact-resistant Coleman 12 oz. Enamel Mug. Each lightweight, steel bowl is dishwasher-safe and coated in a durable, classic blue speckled enamel finish. They also feature rolled rims for sipping comfort.
data.reviewText: Perfect size, but the handle is coming off at the top.
data.text:

Coleman 12 oz. Enamel Mug

DESCRIPTION:
Grab a refreshing drink at the campsite, BBQ or picnic with an impact-resistant Coleman 12 oz. Enamel Mug. Each lightweight, steel bowl is dishwasher-safe and coated in a durable, classic blue speckled enamel finish. They also feature rolled rims for sipping comfort.

data.title: Coleman Montana 6-1239;x739; 6 Person Tent
data.description: The Montana 6 tent from Coleman sleeps 6 campers and includes 1 large 'D' style door for easy entry/exit with a hooded fly to provide ventilation even in rain. A large hooded awning over the front door provides airflow for cross ventilation as well as nice place to lounge out of sun or rain.. Mesh vents on the roof provide additional ventilation and the rugged 1000D polyethylene floor is a bathtub style (wraps up the sides) for extreme weather protection and has welded seams, even in the corners, to guard against a leaky floor.Polyester fly snd wall fabric (450mm coating) are tough and durable..Windows and roof vents for maximum ventilation and comfort.Mesh roof aids in ventilation and reduces condensation.Shockcorded fiberglass frame for easy set up.Polyester fly is tough and durable and won't stretch when wet.1 zippered Electrical Access Port to run AC power into the tent.1 vented Cool Air Port for increased ventilation and comfort.1 interior storage pocket.Pin and ring pole to body connection is fast and simple.Polyethylene welcome mat for muddy shoes.External guy points to keep the tent stable in storms and high winds.Combination clips and continuous pole sleeves for a quick easy set up every time.Instructions sewn to carry bag so they are easy to find and hard to lose.All carry sacks and stakes included.Includes convenient flashlight loop on the ceiling.Fire retardant.Import..
data.reviewText: Great, spacious tent.  Easy up.. Easy down.  Could not, tho, for the life of us, figure out the fly :/

data.title: Coleman Steel-Belted Portable Cooler, 54 Quart
data.description: Go back in time with cold food and drinks today when you take a Coleman 54 Quart Steel Belted Cooler along with you. It looks just like the one you remember with its classic design first developed in 1954. Large enough to hold 85 cans and tall enough to hold 2-liter bottles upright, you'll have plenty of space for refreshments for a whole group of people. Whether you're headed on an overnight camping trip, to a tailgate party, or out to a BBQ, the comfort-grip steel handles and stainless steel lid latch make getting to your destination easy, comfortable and secure. Once you arrive, the Have-A-Seat lid will ensure you always have a place to sit and relax while you enjoy the day with a cold drink in hand. When the fun is done, the leak-resistant channel drain makes it easy to empty any excess water without tilting the cooler, so you're ready for your next fun adventure. A Coleman Steel Belted Cooler will rekindle your fondest outdoor memories  and equip you to make many new ones.
data.reviewText: 3 stars because it looks really cool.  Ice retention, especially on hot days, is terrible.  Forget about using it in the sun on a hot summer day.  Your ice will melt very quickly.  The lid doesn't stay closed very well either.  This is great for fall camping or outdoor parties on cooler days.
data.text:
TITLE:
Coleman Steel-Belted Portable Cooler, 54 Quart

DESCRIPTION:
Go back in time with cold food and drinks today when you take a Coleman 54 Quart Steel Belted Cooler along with you. It looks just like the one you remember with its classic design first developed in 1954. Large enough to hold 85 cans and tall enough to hold 2-liter bottles upright, you'll have plenty of space for refreshments for a whole group of people. Whether you're headed on an overnight camping trip, to a tailgate party, or out to a BBQ, the comfort-grip steel handles and stainless steel lid latch make getting to your destination easy, comfortable and secure. Once you arrive, the Have-A-Seat lid will ensure you always have a place to sit and relax while you enjoy the day with a cold drink in hand. When the fun is done, the leak-resistant channel drain makes it easy to empty any excess water without tilting the cooler, so you're ready for your next fun adventure. A Coleman Steel Belted Cooler will rekindle your fondest outdoor memories  and equip you to make many new ones.

REVIEW:
3 stars because it looks really cool.  Ice retention, especially on hot days, is terrible.  Forget about using it in the sun on a hot summer day.  Your ice will melt very quickly.  The lid doesn't stay closed very well either.  This is great for fall camping or outdoor parties on cooler days.

data.title: Coleman WeatherMaster 6-Person Tent with Screen Room
data.description:
Watch nature unfold before your eyes in Coleman WeatherMaster 6-Person Tent with Screen Room. During the day, the screen room lets you enjoy the great outdoors away from bugs and can be used on warmer nights for extra sleeping space. If it rains, this tent keeps you dry thanks to WeatherTec technology, which uses a tub-like floor with patented corner welds, protected seams, and a covered zipper to help keep water out. Meanwhile, the strong frame is tested to withstand 35+ mph winds. Additionally, an angled window keeps rain out while allowing for extra air circulation. A hinged door makes for easy coming and going. Setup is simple with snag-free, continuous pole sleeves and a patented pin-and-ring system. Inside, there's enough room to stretch out on 2 queen-size airbeds, and storage pockets keep small items organized and off the floor. When it's time to head home, the tent easily packs up into an expandable carry bag with a rip strip for extra storage until you're off on your next adventure.


A patent-pending hinged door provides easy entry and exit, and a touch of at-home convenience.
The Coleman WeatherMaster 6 Tent has two rooms and plenty of space for the entire family. Up to six campers can sleep easily inside thanks to the 17-by-nine (W x D) footprint and 6 ft. 10 in. center height. Two doors�including a patent-pending hinged front door�offer easy in-and-out. An additional floor-less screened room provides extra ventilation on warm days, a great place for meals and gathering, rain or shine, and protected storage space. Coleman's Comfort System allows excellent ventilation control via Variflow adjustable airflow, a vented Cool-Air privacy Port, privacy vent window, and all-mesh ceiling. A convenient electrical access port lets you run a cord from your device in the tent to an outside power source, while an interior gear pocket helps you keep things organized. Setup is quick and simple thanks two color-coded poles and continuous pole sleeves, shock-corded fiberglass poles, an exclusive pin-and-ring design, and InstaClip attachments. Straightforward instructions are sewn into the convenient carry bag, and separate stake and pole bags are also included. Coleman's WeatherTec System
This proprietary series of interworking features keeps the weather where it belongs�outside the tent�at all times.
  • Leak-free Seams: In addition to fully-taped rainfly seams, the rainfly covers doors and windows, and incorporates easy-to-use Velcro frame attachments
  • Weather-resistant Fabric: Coated polyester fabric combined with anti-wicking thread, webbing, and zippers are designed to keep you dry
  • Protected Seams: Inverted floor seams dramatically increase weather resistance by hiding needle holes inside the tent, away from the elements
  • Waterproof Floors: Welding technology strengthens the tent floor and eliminates needle holes
  • Zipper Protection: Zipper cuff adds protection from the elements to the door
  • Wind Strong Frame: Engineered to be a stronger, more wind-responsive frame by using redesigned poles and guy-out triangles to anchor tents and increase performance
Specifications
  • 75-denier polyester taffeta fly
  • 68-denier polyester mesh inner tent
  • 1,000-denier polyethylene floor
  • 19-millimeter steel poles
The Coleman Limited Warranty
Coleman has been in business for more than 100 years, and continues to produce high-quality, affordable tents, lanterns, table-top stoves, and more. All Coleman products have a warranty against defects in material and workmanship, and the WeatherMaster 6 Tent includes a limited one-year manufacturer's warranty. About Coleman Outdoor Products
More than 100 years ago, an entrepreneurial young man named W.C. Coleman had an idea for manufacturing better lanterns in Wichita, Kansas. A century later, Coleman's current catalog features a wide-ranging array of products that make spending time outside a pleasure. The company crafts coolers that keep food and drinks cold for days, airbeds that are comfortable and won't deflate during the night, a complete line of bright and long-lasting LED lights, powerful portable grills that cook with an authentic open-grill flame, and more.
data.reviewText: My wife insisted on having a huge tent that would fit a queen sized air bed and a playpen. This fit the bill, so she ordered it. it didn't take long to assemble, but exoframes went out in the 80s for a good reason, I don't know why Coleman wants to bring them back. The rigid door frame was a nice touch and made it easy to get an out of, but I think the screened area is too small to be useful. There are a lot of parts and stakes, but the instructions are fairly clear. Best of all, it fit back in the bag when I was done.

data.title: Coleman 2000016402 Mess Kit, Aluminum, 5-Pc.
data.description: 5 Piece, Aluminum Mess Kit, Includes 8 OZ Cup, 7" Fry Pan, 16 OZ Pot With Lid & 6.5" Dish Pan, Pieces Nest Inside Each Other & Is Covered With A Lid For Easy Storage & Transport.
data.reviewText:
I'm on a tight budget and thought what's the worst that could happen, these end up being as flimsy as everyone says? Surely I can still cook in them no matter how badly constructed they are..

BZZZZZZZ WRONG. I didn't even bother with these I used my friends camping pots to cook stuff. They are a JOKE. The metal is so thin you could puncture it with a knife, I would never dare put these over something hot then grab them later as it's guaranteed to burn your fingers.

They bend/scratch like no other, and worst part is they awkwardly sit inside of each other and can easily fall out unless you put them in a bag.

DON'T BUY THIS JUNK SAVE YOUR MONEY AND GET SOMETHING BETTER FOR A BIT EXTRA. I mean seriously, don't even make the mistake of buying these to 'get buy' if you're on a tight budget, if you're trying to get by just bring pots from home.

data.title: Coleman Classic Propane Stove
data.description: Coleman Classic Propane Stove About Coleman More than 100 years ago, a young man with an entrepreneurial spirit and a better idea began manufacturing lanterns in Wichita, Kansas. His name was W.C. Coleman, and the company he founded would change life in America. A man plagued with such poor vision he sometimes had to ask classmates to read aloud to him, Coleman saw a brilliant light in 1900 in a drugstore window that stopped him in his tracks. He inquired about the light inside and discovered he was able to read even the small print on a medicine bottle by the illumination. The lamps had mantles, not wicks, and were fueled by gasoline under pressure instead of coal oil. Soon afterward, Coleman started a lighting service that offered a "no light, no pay" clause--a big step forward for merchants who were burned by inferior products that rarely worked--and drew substantial interest from businesses that wanted to keep their lights on after dark. In the ensuing years, Coleman expanded its product line well beyond lanterns. The company's current catalog is thick with products that make spending time outdoors a pleasure. There are coolers that keep food and drinks cold for days on end, comfortable airbeds that won't deflate during the night, a complete line of LED lights that last for years, powerful portable grills that cook with an authentic open-grill flame, and much, much more. Coleman has truly fashioned much of our outdoor camping experience, and expects to do so for generations to come.
data.reviewText: We used this for a short camping trip.  The flame control was not much use.  From all the way on to low was about a sixteenth of a turn, if that much.  Hard to lower to a simmer, without it going out.  Very poor.  Other then that a good stove.
data.text:
TITLE:
Coleman Classic Propane Stove

DESCRIPTION:
Coleman Classic Propane Stove About Coleman More than 100 years ago, a young man with an entrepreneurial spirit and a better idea began manufacturing lanterns in Wichita, Kansas. His name was W.C. Coleman, and the company he founded would change life in America. A man plagued with such poor vision he sometimes had to ask classmates to read aloud to him, Coleman saw a brilliant light in 1900 in a drugstore window that stopped him in his tracks. He inquired about the light inside and discovered he was able to read even the small print on a medicine bottle by the illumination. The lamps had mantles, not wicks, and were fueled by gasoline under pressure instead of coal oil. Soon afterward, Coleman started a lighting service that offered a "no light, no pay" clause--a big step forward for merchants who were burned by inferior products that rarely worked--and drew substantial interest from businesses that wanted to keep their lights on after dark. In the ensuing years, Coleman expanded its product line well beyond lanterns. The company's current catalog is thick with products that make spending time outdoors a pleasure. There are coolers that keep food and drinks cold for days on end, comfortable airbeds that won't deflate during the night, a complete line of LED lights that last for years, powerful portable grills that cook with an authentic open-grill flame, and much, much more. Coleman has truly fashioned much of our outdoor camping experience, and expects to do so for generations to come.

REVIEW:
We used this for a short camping trip.  The flame control was not much use.  From all the way on to low was about a sixteenth of a turn, if that much.  Hard to lower to a simmer, without it going out.  Very poor.  Other then that a good stove.

data.title: Coleman C003 Soft Backpack Cooler
data.description: Keep your food and snacks cool from the park to the campsite with a Coleman Backpack Cooler. This stylish bag is large enough to hold 28 cans. Extra padding in the back and waist make it even easier to carry over almost any distance. The BPA- and phthalate-free, heat-welded main compartment is perfect for using with ice and the zippered front pocket lets you store extra snacks and small gear. The bungee cord on the front also provides quick access to other items. Antimicrobial, product-protecting properties are built into the flexible lining to resist odor, mold and mildew, making it easy to clean between uses.
data.reviewText:
It is well made.
I use backpacks like this to bring groceries home on my bike.
I wish someone would make a bigger one, but this is the best one I have found that is a cooler.

data.title: Coleman Micro Packer LED Lantern, 100 L
data.description: Grab a lantern and a flashlight in the palm of your hand with the Coleman MicroPacker LED Lantern. The bright, 2-in-1 design snaps from area lighting to directional lighting quite literally in a snap. The bright LED lasts a lifetime, so it never needs to be replaced. The versatile light lasts up to 92 hours, and an extremely lightweight design is only 6 oz.-even with the three AA-cell batteries that power the lantern.
data.reviewText:
DOES NOT convert to a flashlight. No way, no how. Just like the old Coleman gasoline lanterns had a metal reflector that could shield one side? This has an inch-wide black (!) plastic barrier that slides up, so it doesn't shine back in your eyes. That doesn't make it into a "flashlight" in any way. It doesn't focus or concentrate the light in any way. It just blocks a narrow strip from your eyes.

Other than that, it looks decently made, and the 3xAA-or-1xD battery feature is nice. But I was expecting something like the Eveready lights, that pop up to be a lantern, and pop down to be a focused flashlight. This isn't/

data.title: Coleman Camp Axe
data.description: Get the right tool for multiple jobs when you're at the campsite with the Coleman Camp Axe. The sharp edge of the durable drop-forged steel head easily splits wood for a campfire or stove, while the flat edge works wonders for driving tent stakes into hard ground. When it's time to pack up, pulling your tent stakes is easy with the notch cut into the head. Durable and well built, the Coleman Camp Axe is perfect for the campsite, wooded terrain, or the backyard. The axe is equipped with a drop-forged carbon-steel axe head that easily splits wood into kindling, along with a forged steel handle and an ergonomic nonslip grip. With the Coleman Camp Axe, you'll be ready for any camping contingency.
data.reviewText: This little ax is just what I need for backpacking.  It's small, but nice and sturdy.  It works great for driving in the stakes of my tent but when I went to gather firewood I realized the ax had no cutting edge to speak of.  A 3" thick tree would've taken hours to cut down.  If you purchase this ax be sure to buy a sharpening stone as well because aside from driving in tent stakes its pretty much useless out of the box.  Once it's sharpened up it works nicely but be sure to sharpen it before you find yourself in the middle of nowhere with a dull useless ax.
data.text:
TITLE:
Coleman Camp Axe

DESCRIPTION:
Get the right tool for multiple jobs when you're at the campsite with the Coleman Camp Axe. The sharp edge of the durable drop-forged steel head easily splits wood for a campfire or stove, while the flat edge works wonders for driving tent stakes into hard ground. When it's time to pack up, pulling your tent stakes is easy with the notch cut into the head. Durable and well built, the Coleman Camp Axe is perfect for the campsite, wooded terrain, or the backyard. The axe is equipped with a drop-forged carbon-steel axe head that easily splits wood into kindling, along with a forged steel handle and an ergonomic nonslip grip. With the Coleman Camp Axe, you'll be ready for any camping contingency.

REVIEW:
This little ax is just what I need for backpacking.  It's small, but nice and sturdy.  It works great for driving in the stakes of my tent but when I went to gather firewood I realized the ax had no cutting edge to speak of.  A 3" thick tree would've taken hours to cut down.  If you purchase this ax be sure to buy a sharpening stone as well because aside from driving in tent stakes its pretty much useless out of the box.  Once it's sharpened up it works nicely but be sure to sharpen it before you find yourself in the middle of nowhere with a dull useless ax.

data.title: Coleman Bottle Top Propane Stove,Green,6.62" H x 7.81" W x 7.75" L
data.description:
Need a lightweight outdoor stove for backpacking, hunting, or camping? Look no further than the Coleman Portable Bottle-Top Propane Stove, which fits up to an 8-inch pan above a fully adjustable burner for precise temperature control and up to 10,000 BTUs of cooking power. Brew coffee, heat up hot dogs, and so on, knowing you can enjoy up to 2.5 hours of cooking on just one 16.4 oz. propane cylinder (sold separately). PerfectFlow technology keeps that heat steady, even in extreme conditions, and a large base offers stability for easy stirring. When its time to break down the campsite and fit all your gear into a crowded pack, youll be relieved to know that the burner and base separate from the propane cylinder for truly compact packing. Rest assured your ultra-portable stove comes with a 3-year limited warranty. The Coleman 5431A700 One-burner Propane Stove is an easy-to-use portable stove that should meet almost any camp cooking need. The PerfectFlow regulator provides consistent cooking performance by producing a steady fuel stream, even in cold weather, high altitudes, or when fuel is low. Equipped with one 10,000 BTU burner, this fully adjustable stove will last for 2.2 hours on high or up to nine hours on low. The pot supports help shield your cooking flame from the wind, while the large eight-inch burner bowl should fit most any pot. This Coleman one-burner stove can boil a quart of water in just four minutes! This stove operates on one 16.4-ounce cylinder of Coleman propane (not included). Manufacturer Warranty
Limited lifetime warranty
data.reviewText: While this stove creates plenty of heat and will do so under wet, windy conditions. The few times i have used it, when I was finished with the stove, and removed the gas cylinder for transport. The Cylinder SEALS were compromised and leaked until the cylinder was EMPTY. I have several other stoves which use the same cylinders and NONE of them compromise the cylinder seals. Dangerous waste of fuel and a definite weakness in stove function..

data.title: Coleman Self-Inflating Camping Pad with Pillow
data.description: Enjoy additional support and cushioning with the Coleman Self-Inflating Camping Pad with Pillow. This camping sleeping pad features a comfy tufted design that adds an extra layer of padding between your sleeping bag and the ground. Equipped with a free-flow valve, the sleeping pad self-inflates with no air pump needed. Simply roll it out and open the valve, and the pad will begin inflating. When it's time to pack up, reopen the valve and push the air out using the compression straps. Thanks to its durable, weather-resistant polyester shell, this camping sleeping pad can endure night after night of use.
data.reviewText: This pad looks good when it blows up automatically. It is hard to roll it back. Also the straps and very poorly designed when rolling it back to original shape. The strap broke from the first use. Did not work for me.

data.title: Coleman Dome Tent with Screen Room | Evanston Camping Tent with Screened-In Porch
data.description: Enjoy the great outdoors without the bugs inside the Coleman Evanston 8-Person Tent with Screen Room. Great for car camping and extended camping trips, this spacious tent offers enough room for 2 queen-size airbeds and is designed to set up in under 15 minutes thanks to snag-free, continuous pole sleeves and Insta-Clip pole attachments. The tents WeatherTec system features patented welded floors and inverted seams to keep the rain off you and your gear; a rainfly is included for extra weather protection. Extended awnings let you keep the windows open for enhanced air circulation without letting in the rain. The tent also comes with a separate screened-in room for extra ventilation, covered storage for your gear, or an extra sleeping area on warmer, dry nights. Remove the included rainfly to let in more sunlight through the mesh roof on dry days or to gaze up at the stars on dry nights. "
data.reviewText: On our second use, the fabric where the pole goes through ripped. On our 3rd use, zipper broke and we cant even close the tent anymore. Emailed Coleman multiple times but they won't get back to us. Now the tent is useless and not sure if Coleman will replace this tent even though we still have our one year warranty.

data.title: Coleman Propane Grill | RoadTrip LXE Portable Gas Grill
data.description: Stop manhandling those heavy, bulky backyard beasts and show off as the grill master with the Coleman RoadTrip LXE Propane Grill. It's easy to tote around and set up. Just fold it up, roll it with one hand to your favorite cooking spot and set it up in a matter of seconds with the lift and lock system. The grill comes equipped with durable cast-iron grilling surfaces. (Coleman RoadTrip pot support and griddles are available separately.) When you're ready to grill, push a button to ignite the InstaStart matchless lighting system. Cook for more than an hour on one propane cylinder with 20,000 BTUs of power on 285 sq. in. (1838.7 sq. cm) of cooking surface. It's perfect for BBQs, picnics, tailgating, camping or a night on the patio. When you're ready to leave, it folds down small enough to fit in the back of most car trunks.
data.reviewText: As far as grill quality goes, don't expect much. The cooking is uneven. But it's very portable and good for camping.

data.title: Coleman Quad LED Lantern Special Edition Ultra Bright 190 Lumens, Red
data.description:
Take the convenience of four lights in one with you with the Coleman Quad LED Lantern. Together the lantern shines 190 ultra-bright lumens for up to 75 hours. During an emergency, pop one of the four lights off the base for a personal flashlight. They recharge once theyre back on the base for hands-free light. The 24 LEDs last a lifetime, so they never need replacing and the lantern runs cool so its always safe to touch, even when its been on for hours. Combining an area light with four portable lanterns in a single clever unit, the Coleman LED Quad lantern is a versatile choice for indoor or outdoor use. The Quad is equipped with four removable LED panels that hold 24 LED lights total. Users can remove any of the four panels and use them independently, such as when needing a portable light for the bathroom or tent, or they can keep all four attached to the lantern and produce 190 total lumens of light. As a result, the Quad is the perfect light for group camping trips and any other occasion where folks might splinter off into smaller groups during the night. Each of the panels is a fully functioning light, with six 5mm white LEDs, a rechargeable NiMH battery, an independent on/off switch, and a top handle for carrying or hanging. The NiMH battery--which recharges when stored in the lantern base--provides up to 1.5 hours of light on its own. The lantern base, meanwhile, requires eight D-cell batteries (not included), which provide an approximate run time of 75 hours for the entire lantern. Other features include a main on/off switch located on top of the lantern, a light range of 26 feet, and a limited five-year warranty. About Coleman
More than 100 years ago, a young man with an entrepreneurial spirit and a better idea began manufacturing lanterns in Wichita, Kansas. His name was W.C. Coleman, and the company he founded would change life in America. A man plagued with such poor vision he sometimes had to ask classmates to read aloud to him, Coleman saw a brilliant light in 1900 in a drugstore window that stopped him in his tracks. He inquired about the light inside and discovered he was able to read even the small print on a medicine bottle by the illumination. The lamps had mantles, not wicks, and were fueled by gasoline under pressure instead of coal oil. Soon afterward, Coleman started a lighting service that offered a "no light, no pay" clause--a big step forward for merchants who were burned by inferior products that rarely worked--and drew substantial interest from businesses that wanted to keep their lights on after dark. In the ensuing years, Coleman expanded its product line well beyond lanterns. The company's current catalog is thick with products that make spending time outdoors a pleasure. There are coolers that keep food and drinks cold for days on end, comfortable airbeds that won't deflate during the night, a complete line of LED lights that last for years, powerful portable grills that cook with an authentic open-grill flame, and much, much more. Coleman has truly fashioned much of our outdoor camping experience, and expects to do so for generations to come.
data.reviewText: This lamp has made multiple camping trips and has always been reliable. Through good and bad weather. It is very handy as you can use it as a whole unit - as pictured - and as individual panels (four in total) that each family member can use. Overall, good product.

Through good and bad weather. It is very handy as you can use it as a whole unit - as pictured - and as individual panels (four in total) that each family member can use. Overall, good product.

data.title: Coleman Adjustable Comfort 30 Degree Sleeping Bag
data.reviewText: My husband and I had high hopes with this sleeping bag but soon realized that it was not going to work. First of all it was too short for my husband who is 6 feet tall. Secondly, we plan on camping in cold weather and this sleeping bag did not feel warm enough. The sheet was espcially thin. It looked good but was not going to work for us.

data.title: Coleman 9-Can Soft Cooler with Removable Liner
data.description: Keep food and drinks cold with the Coleman Soft Cooler. This compact, portable cooler bag holds up to nine cans and offers plenty of space for a family picnic or game-day snacks. Heat-welded seams in the zippered main compartment prevent leaks and maintain a cold environment, while the removable hard plastic liner fits snugly inside the soft cooler and allows you to easily dump out any ice melt. When not using the hard plastic liner, you can use ice substitutes to keep contents cold and dry. The cooler bag's easy-clean interior has an antimicrobial lining to resist formation of mold, mildew, fungus, and odor. A zippered front pocket, pocket under the lid, and two side mesh pockets make it easy to carry water bottles, snacks, silverware, and other accessories. You can also secure items to the top with the convenient bungee cords built into the lid. An adjustable shoulder strap lets you comfortably carry the cooler anywhere you go.
data.reviewText: Too small but a nice bag

data.title: Coleman Soft Plush Top Inflated Quickbed
data.description: Take the comfort of an indoor bed with you when you sleep outside on the Coleman Queen Airbed Folding Cot with Side Tables and 4D Battery Pump. Connect the included 4D pump to the Double Lock valve for easy, leak-free inflation. Once you find the perfect firmness, the AirTight system will help keep it that way through the night, since it's factory-tested to be leak-free. The ComfortStrong coil system provides all-night support. It is more than a place to sleep with 2 side tables with cup holders to easily place your drink, a snack, a magazine, or personal items on both sides of the bed. A sewn-in cover keeps the airbed in place on the strong steel frame. When youre ready to pack up, the Double Lock valve lets you deflate the airbed just as easily, and it folds up into a carry bag for easy transport and storage.
data.reviewText: To easily punctured. Lasted for only one camp trip :(

data.title: Coleman Motorcycle Helmet Bag
data.description: This gorgeous MadDog Gear motorcycle helmet bag has the finest details and highest quality you will find anywhere! MadDog Gear motorcycle helmet bag is truly remarkable. Product details:  condition: brand new  item SKU: ss-sms-4003578  information: your helmet keeps you safe on your bike; now it's your turn to keep it safe from bumps and scratches with a motorcycle helmet bag designed by Paul Jr. Himself. The heavy-duty, water-resistant polyester bag fits most street bike helmets, and the reinforced leather-like bottom adds extra protection and stops the case from sliding. Inside, the fleece lining keeps your helmet warm and adds extra protection from scratches and other damage.
data.reviewText:
My BRP moduar 3 helmet didn't fit in this bag so i use it for an open face helmet with a sheild.
Really is a nice bag for the price.
